title: Re: MAP & timeline for evaluation materials exchange
Review: AWS Marketplace listing commission & upcoming renewal
preview:
From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alicia Gomez <alicia.gomez@aws-partners.com>
Cc:  
Date: 2026-05-10T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Review: AWS Marketplace listing commission & upcoming renewal

Hi Alicia,

Hope you’re well. Our AWS listing renewal window is coming up in late June and we just received a notice about an updated commission/fee schedule for Marketplace listings. I’ve attached our current listing summary for context. Could you confirm: 

- What exactly changes on July 1 (commission rates, transaction fees, processing)
- Whether existing listings are grandfathered or move to the new tier immediately
- If any promotional/marketing co-op or credits are available to offset the uplift

We’re trying to finalize our FY26 reseller economics and need a concrete position to present to finance and partnerships. Quick answers would be extremely helpful — happy to hop on a call if it’s easier.

Thanks,
Laura Bennett
Sr. Manager, Partnerships
Redwood Inference
[Attachment: Redwood_Current_Marketplace_Listing.pdf]

From: Alicia Gomez <alicia.gomez@aws-partners.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marketplace Billing <marketplace-billing@amazon.com>
Date: 2026-05-10T11:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Review: AWS Marketplace listing commission & upcoming renewal

Hi Laura — thanks for the note and the listing doc. Short answers below and I’ve attached the fee schedule we circulated last month.

- Effective July 1 we’re introducing a tiered commission structure: Base 9% for standard SaaS listings, 6% for qualified ISV Accelerate partners, and volume discounts beyond $5M ARR.
- Existing listings are NOT automatically grandfathered; partners have a 60-day window to opt into transitional terms that can cap increases for 12 months.
- We do have limited marketing co-op credits for partners that meet the Accelerate criteria (joint webinars, featured tile). I’ve flagged your listing for review.

If you’d like, I can provisionally offer a 6-week extension on the transitional cap while we evaluate Accelerate eligibility — would need a short SOW + sign-off from your procurement to lock.

Best,
Alicia Gomez
Partner Programs – AWS Marketplace
[Attachment: AWS_Marketplace_Fee_Schedule_v2.pdf]

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc:  
Date: 2026-05-10T13:03:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Review: AWS Marketplace listing commission & upcoming renewal

Forwarding Alicia’s reply — key points below. Rafael / Stephanie, please advise on negotiation posture:

- Alicia says listings aren’t automatically grandfathered but there’s a 60-day window for transitional terms that cap increases for 12 months.
- Possible marketing co-op if we meet Accelerate criteria; she’s flagged our listing for review.
- Option for a 6-week extension while we execute a short SOW (Procurement involvement needed).

Can we:
- Request grandfathering for our current rate for 12 months instead of just a cap?
- Ask for a guaranteed marketing co-op budget tied to the FY26 roadmap (co-marketing + featured placement)?
- Confirm who signs the SOW on our side — procurement or partnerships?

I’ve attached the current SOW template procurement uses for partner programs. Rafael — your ask / negotiation bullets here would be great.

Thanks,
Laura
[Attachment: Procurement_SOW_v3.docx]

--- Forwarded message ---
From: Alicia Gomez <alicia.gomez@aws-partners.com>
Date: 2026-05-10T11:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Review: AWS Marketplace listing commission & upcoming renewal
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marketplace Billing <marketplace-billing@amazon.com>

(see above)

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc:  
Date: 2026-05-10T15:20:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Review: AWS Marketplace listing commission & upcoming renewal

Short notes — negotiating posture:

- Priority 1: Keep effective take-rate unchanged for FY26. Ask for grandfathering of current terms for 12 months; if not, demand a hard cap at current rate.
- Priority 2: Secure a fixed marketing co-op (dollar amount or guaranteed placements) tied to the listing — this offsets the fee delta.
- Priority 3: If AWS requires the SOW, limit its scope to marketing and listing maintenance, no new commercial terms baked in. Legal to sign only after procurement reviews.

If Alicia is willing to offer the 6-week extension while we finalize, we’ll use that window to get legal/procurement approval. Laura — please request the extension formally and ask Alicia for the Partner Program SLA/eligibility checklist.

Rafael
Sr. Director, Sales Operations
Redwood Inference

On May 10, 2026, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Forwarding Alicia’s reply — key points below. Rafael / Stephanie, please advise on negotiation posture:
> ...

(quoted for context)

From: Alicia Gomez <alicia.gomez@aws-partners.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marketplace Billing <marketplace-billing@amazon.com>
Date: 2026-05-11T10:47:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Review: AWS Marketplace listing commission & upcoming renewal

Thanks for the quick turnaround, Laura and Rafael. Good news — we can offer the following provisional terms while you complete your internal approvals: 

- 12-month transitional cap at your current effective rate if you sign the short SOW by 2026-06-01.
- Marketing co-op: up to $25k in co-funded campaigns + one featured tile in Marketplace for a 90-day period (Accelerate eligibility dependent).
- We will require a counter-signed SOW scoped to marketing activities and listing maintenance only. No changes to underlying contract language besides the fee cap addendum.

I’ve looped Marketplace Billing to prepare an invoice template and added a draft SOW stub. If you want, I can set a 30-minute slot tomorrow to review the SOW and eligibility checklist.

Best,
Alicia
Partner Programs – AWS Marketplace
[Attachment: AWS_Marketplace_Fee_Schedule_v2.pdf]

From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alicia Gomez <alicia.gomez@aws-partners.com>, Marketplace Billing <marketplace-billing@amazon.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-05-12T16:03:00-07:00
Subject: Request: Formal amendment & timeline for AWS Marketplace fee cap

Alicia / Billing team — thanks for the proposed transitional cap and marketing co-op. To move forward we need a formal amendment document and a short timeline that our procurement and legal teams can review. Our expectations:

- A one-page amendment that: 1) explicitly states the 12-month cap at the current effective rate, 2) references the Marketplace fee schedule version and effective date, and 3) confirms the marketing co-op commitment (amount and deliverables).
- A draft SOW limited to marketing/listing tasks only; Redwood legal will not accept any commercial or indemnity changes in the SOW.
- 60-day extension on the renewal window while we review the amendment (we will aim to sign within 30 days).

Please send the amendment to contracts@redwoodinference.com and copy my team. If Marketplace Billing needs a purchase order or invoice to reserve the co-op funds, send the template and we’ll get procurement to process it quickly.

Thanks — Vivek Kulkarni
Head of Partnerships
Redwood Inference

[Attachment: Procurement_SOW_v3.docx]

--- End of thread ---

title: Question on multi-region pricing split and compliance surcharge
preview:
From: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@acmehealth.eu>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-05-05T09:12:00Z
Subject: Clarify US vs EU pricing + residency adders

Hi David,

Thanks for the demo last week — the team liked the latency profile and the routing options. Before we lock an internal budget, procurement needs a clear view on how Redwood applies multi-region pricing. A few concrete questions: 

- Do you have a single hosted API rate card or are there separate per-region rates?
- Is there a fixed “data residency” or “EU add-on” applied per account, per request, or as a percentage adder to per-token rates?
- For an estimated steady-state of ~120M tokens/month (expected 60% US, 40% EU), how do charges appear on invoices? Any examples helpful.
- What discounts are available for committed spend (12mo / 24mo)?

We are also evaluating whether EU-resident data requires a technical routing guarantee (EU-only compute + KV cache). If that has additional fees, please call that out.

Can you send the baseline hosted API rate card and any adders for residency/compliance? Procurement wants a clear, line-item format so they can model TCO.

Best,
Elena
Director of Digital Products
Acme Health



From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
To: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@acmehealth.eu>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-05-05T13:30:00Z
Subject: Re: Clarify US vs EU pricing + residency adders

Elena — thanks for the clear list. Looping in Rafael (sales lead) and Marissa (pricing/finance) who will surface the formal rate card. A couple quick clarifying Qs to make the quote accurate:

- Do you expect the 60/40 split to be stable or spike-seasonal (e.g., monthly bursts)?
- Any specific compliance requirements (SOC 2 type II is default; do you need HIPAA, PCI, or regional certifications)?
- Will Acme require BYOK/KMS for keys in the EU or is hosted-managed keys OK?

Rafael — can you attach the current hosted API rate card and a sample invoice layout. Marissa — if there are standard residency waivers or commit tiers that offset region adders, please include.

I’ll also include a draft SOW that calls out geo-routing and caching behavior (EU-only compute + fallback).

Thanks,
David
Account Executive | Redwood Inference


From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
To: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@acmehealth.eu>, David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-05-06T11:02:00Z
Subject: Re: Clarify US vs EU pricing + residency adders

Elena — appreciate the detail. Attaching our May 2026 hosted API rate card and a draft SOW that calls out geo-routing behavior. Highlights / proposal below (baseline hosted API, list pricing before discounts):

- Baseline hosted API (US region): $0.00060 per input token, $0.00120 per output token. Single consolidated invoice by account.
- EU region residency adder: +15% applied to per-token rates for traffic served from EU-hosted endpoints (applies at request/compute level).
- Dedicated/private deployments (VPC): additional MRC — typically $6,000/mo for a small pool + per-GPU compute; residency adder treated case-by-case.
- Committed spend discounts (applied to total monthly bill across regions): 6mo commit = 8% off list, 12mo = 14% off, 24mo = 20% off. Discounts stack with volume tiers (>= $100k/month triggers higher tier).
- Waiver option: for 24mo committed agreements with > $200k ARR, we can negotiate partial or full waiving of the EU residency adder for the contract term.

Routing/technical notes:
- EU residency adders are triggered when a request is routed to EU compute. Our smart routing can pin data (and KV cache) to EU-only nodes; fallback to a non-EU node is possible but would be flagged and priced at the non-EU rate unless you opt-in for strict residency (which blocks fallback).
- BYOK for hosted API: supported for enterprise customers for an additional management fee and KMS integration work; true HSM/BYOK on-prem is available under Private.

I’ve attached: redwood-ratecard-may2026.xlsx and acme-multi-region-sow-draft.pdf. DocuSign link included if you want to start a term sheet.

Next steps: if you can confirm the expected US/EU token split and a target commit length, we’ll produce a firm quote with line items and an invoice example.

Best,
Rafael Mendes
Senior Sales Manager | Redwood Inference


From: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@acmehealth.eu>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-05-12T09:05:00Z
Subject: Re: Clarify US vs EU pricing + residency adders

Thanks Rafael — that’s very helpful. A few follow-ups from procurement and security: 

- The +15% EU adder is larger than we expected. Is Redwood open to an 8% adder if we commit 12 months at ~120M tokens/month? Alternatively, can you offer a quarterly credit back for actual EU traffic to offset part of the adder?
- We will require SOC 2 type II evidence and an exportable audit for KMS usage. We may also need a short pilot (30 days) with a capped spend to validate routing.
- Can you confirm whether the residency adder is visible as a separate line item on invoices? Procurement prefers per-region line items.

If you can provide a revised quote with (a) 12mo commit pricing showing an 8% EU adder OR (b) 15% adder + quarterly credit schedule, we’ll take it upstream. Also confirm whether strict EU residency (no fallback) is an add-on or a config toggle and whether there is an availability SLA impact.

Regards,
Elena


From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
To: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@acmehealth.eu>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-05-12T15:04:00Z
Subject: Re: Clarify US vs EU pricing + residency adders

Elena — quick summary and proposed next steps:

1) Rafael will run two quote scenarios: (A) 12mo commit with EU adder reduced to 8% and (B) 15% EU adder with a quarterly credit schedule.
2) Marissa to confirm how residency appears on the invoice (we can provide per-region line items).
3) Security will package SOC 2 artifacts and KMS audit export instructions — Rafael, I’ll get you the contact in security to coordinate.
4) We’ll schedule a 30-min pricing call for Thursday 2026-05-14 to finalize numbers and pilot terms.

Can you do Thursday 10:00-10:30 CEST? If yes I’ll send a calendar invite and attach the two quotes once ready.

Thanks,
David
Account Executive | Redwood Inference

title: Elliott Systems - flexible 2yr prepay options and ramp breakpoints
preview:
From: Elly Ryan <elly@elliottsys.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 4 May 2026 09:15:12 -0700
Subject: 2yr prepay — need flexibility on annual ramps / split billing

Rafael,

Thanks again for the product demo last week — the team at Elliott is excited about the throughput and the KV-cache savings. We are evaluating a 24-month prepay to lock in unit economics, but Procurement has two asks before we can move to legal:

1) Can Redwood support an annual re-opener (yearly true-up) so we can adjust the second-year commitment if our volume ramps faster/slower? We expect uneven growth across Q3–Q6.
2) We need split billing options: an 80/20 non-refundable allocation at signing (to protect Redwood) with the remainder billed annually with a true-up credit or top-up.

High level target: net effective discount in the teens (15–18%) vs monthly list, but we need flexibility (cap on downside credits). Also, please confirm SLA and if underusage credits can be carried forward or refunded.

We'd like a formal proposal and a sample SOW we can share with Procurement. Happy to schedule a quick call if helpful.

—Elly
Elly Ryan
Head of Cloud Platform
Elliott Systems

Attachments: none


From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
To: Elly Ryan <elly@elliottsys.com>
Date: Mon, 4 May 2026 11:04:20 -07:00
Subject: Re: 2yr prepay — need flexibility on annual ramps / split billing

Elly,

Appreciate the clarity — I put together three workable structures that balance Elliott's flexibility needs and Redwood's revenue certainty. See quick options below; I'll attach a worked pricing model and a placeholder SOW after I run this by RevOps.

Option A — Standard 2yr prepay (simplest)
- Full 24-month prepay up front
- 18% off list units (fixed)
- No annual re-opener; credits only in limited circumstances

Option B — Annualized prepay with re-opener (preferred middle ground)
- 50% of contracted spend billed at signing, remainder billed at start of Year 2
- Annual true-up at each anniversary (30-day window) — underusage converted to credits applied to the next 12 months, up to 20% carry-forward cap
- Discount schedule: Year 1 = 15% off list, Year 2 = 17% off list (net blended ~16%)
- Minimum annual breakpoints: 60% of year target to avoid large refunds; otherwise credit applied as above

Option C — Flexible prepay with protected floor (most flexible for you)
- 30% non-refundable at signing, quarterly invoicing thereafter against committed run-rate
- True-up each quarter with annual settlement
- Discount floor 12% / upside to 18% if volume growth >35% YoY (incentivizes ramp)

Notes:
- SLA terms remain the same across options.
- Refunds are limited — we prefer credits because they align incentives and reduce churn.

If you like option B, I'll loop in Priyom (pricing) and Marissa (billing/finance) for sign-off and attach the pricing model + SOW draft. Can we target a response by EOB Wednesday PT?

Thanks,
Rafael Mendes
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
rafael.mendes@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0142

Attachments: (to follow)


From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
To: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: Mon, 4 May 2026 11:07:03 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Elliott Systems - flexible 2yr prepay options and ramp breakpoints — need pricing/finance sign-off

Team — forwarding Elliott's asks and my proposed options (see thread below). Key asks from Elliott: annual re-opener / true-up, split billing (partial non-refundable), and a credit carry-forward cap. I've attached the pricing-model-elliott.xlsx and a draft SOW.

Recommendation for internal approval: pursue Option B as the default. It gives Elliott the annual flexibility they want while keeping most revenue contracted. Ask: can RevOps / Finance confirm if a 20% carry-forward cap and a 60% annual breakpoint are acceptable? Also need a legal-approved refund/credit clause that prevents large cash refunds.

Attachments: pricing-model-elliott.xlsx, sow-elliott-v1.pdf

Thanks,
Rafael


From: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 4 May 2026 14:32:11 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Elliott Systems - flexible 2yr prepay options and ramp breakpoints — need pricing/finance sign-off

Rafael — thanks for the write-up and the model. My thoughts:

- Option B is the right baseline. We can accept 50/50 billing with annual true-up, but tighten the breakpoint language: minimum acceptable annual consumption for Year N must be 65% of that year's committed volume to avoid a cash refund — anything below converts to credits (no refunds).
- Reduce carry-forward cap to 15% of the committed spend (helps revenue recognition and reduces balance sheet credits).
- Discount schedule: Year1 14.5% / Year2 17.5% (rounded in the contract to 14.5% and 17.5%) — gives Elliott real value while preserving margin.
- Require a termination fee if Elliott elects to exit early without a material breach (>6 months of remaining contract => prorated termination fee equal to remaining committed months * 70% of monthly rate).

Marissa, does Finance/legal have any red lines here? If not, I'll approve the attached SOW with these terms. Also suggest we include a limited refund clause only in cases of SLA failure exceeding 99.5% uptime over a 90-day window.

-Priyom
Senior Sales Manager | Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 5 May 2026 09:08:45 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Elliott Systems - flexible 2yr prepay options and ramp breakpoints — need pricing/finance sign-off

Agree with Priyom with two small tweaks from billing/finance perspective:

1) Carry-forward credits: 15% cap ok, but credits older than 12 months expire (no indefinite liability).
2) Split billing wording: "non-refundable" is fine for 50% at signing, but recommend we structure as 40% irrevocable / 10% held as refundable credit applied at Year 1 true-up to reduce risk. This meets Procurement halfway and reduces our cash exposure.

Legal will draft a short clause for early termination (Priyom's suggestion) and the SLA-triggered refund. Once legal returns that language, Rafael — please share the revised SOW and I'll do a final finance sign-off.

Thanks,
Marissa Cole
VP, Revenue Operations | Redwood Inference


From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
To: Elly Ryan <elly@elliottsys.com>, procurement <procurement@elliottsys.com>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 7 May 2026 08:41:02 -07:00
Subject: Proposal: 2-yr annualized prepay with re-opener (Elliott Systems)

Elly / Procurement — thanks for the patience. Per our internal review, please find the proposed structure (attached SOW + pricing model):

Summary (Option B — final):
- Billing: 40% non-refundable at signing, 10% held as refundable credit applied at Year 1 true-up, 50% invoiced at start of Year 2.
- Annual true-up: 30-day window following each anniversary. Underusage converts to credits applied to next 12 months; carry-forward cap = 15% of contracted annual spend, credits expire after 12 months.
- Breakpoint: Minimum annual consumption = 65% of that year's committed volume to avoid an early termination or other remedy.
- Discounts: Year 1 = 14.5% off list; Year 2 = 17.5% off list.
- Early exit: If Elliott terminates with >6 months remaining without breach, a prorated termination fee applies (70% of remaining monthly rates).
- SLA refunds: limited to explicit SLA credits where uptime <99.5% over a 90-day rolling window; not a general cash refund mechanism.

I attached the updated pricing-model-elliott.xlsx and a draft SOW (sow-elliott-v1.pdf). If that aligns, I'll ask Legal/Contracts to issue a DocuSign for signature. Happy to hop on a call tomorrow if you want to walk the SOW.

Thanks — Rafael
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
rafael.mendes@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0142

Attachments: pricing-model-elliott.xlsx, sow-elliott-v1.pdf


From: Elly Ryan <elly@elliottsys.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, procurement <procurement@elliottsys.com>
Date: Thu, 7 May 2026 16:12:04 -07:00
Subject: Re: Proposal: 2-yr annualized prepay with re-opener (Elliott Systems)

Rafael — this is very helpful and close to what Procurement will accept. Two quick asks before we route to Legal: 

1) Can the carry-forward cap be tested with a one-time exception if Elliott under-indexes in Year 1 by more than 40% due to a force-majeure-like event (we can provide written justification)? We'd like a one-time small exception path.
2) Please confirm that the held 10% refundable credit is applied before any termination fee calculation.

If you can confirm those two items in writing, Procurement is comfortable moving this to Legal for DocuSign. Thanks for moving quickly on this.

—Elly

On Thu, May 7, 2026 at 08:41 AM Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com> wrote:
> [attached pricing-model-elliott.xlsx and sow-elliott-v1.pdf]

title: Event collateral + demo script — CloudConf regional slot
preview:
From: Amy Chen <amy@eventworks.co>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.com>, Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 3 May 2026 09:22:00 -0700
Subject: Collateral & demo needs for CloudConf regional — quick checklist

Hi Hannah / Dev,

Thanks again for committing to the regional CloudConf track in Austin (May 14). Devon asked me to consolidate collateral expectations so production can route files to printing and CMS. Can you confirm we can deliver the following by EOD Wednesday (May 6)?

- Final one-pager (4-up rack card PDF, bleed-ready) — one-sided + 2nd-side blurb about Redwood Dedicated
- Short customer case study (1 page) we can print as a handout and post to the event site
- Speaker slide deck (master PPTX, 16:9) — final review requested by May 10
- Demo script / run-of-show (3–4 minute demo; callouts for slide timestamps)

Quick notes: we have a 20-minute slot including Q&A. A/V will support screen-share and laptop HDMI. Estimated print run: 300 copies.

Please attach file stubs (PDF/PPT/MD) in replies. If you need help with the case study copy I can loop in a freelance writer (fee covered by sponsor budget).

Thanks,
Amy Chen
Senior Producer, EventWorks
amy@eventworks.co | +1 415-555-0142

--
CloudConf contact: Devon Martinez <devon@cloudconf.com>

To: Amy Chen <amy@eventworks.co>

Date: Sun, 3 May 2026 11:05:00 -0700

Hi Amy — thanks for the checklist and timeline. We can hit the May 6 deliverable for the one-pager and the draft demo script; final slide deck by May 10 should be fine.

Attached: [attachment: CloudConf_onepager_v2.pdf (PDF) — 1-up rack card draft]

Highlights / asks from Redwood:
- One-pager: please advise if the print vendor needs crop/trim marks; the attached is export-ready but I didn’t add printer marks.
- Case study: Monica (cc’d) is working on the Acme case study copy — Monica, ETA for first draft?
- Demo script: I’ve sketched the outline below. Dev, can you convert to run-of-show with callouts for slide timestamps?

Draft demo outline (3.5 mins):
1) 0:00–0:20 — Slide 1: quick company intro and slot greeting
2) 0:20–1:10 — Slide 2–3: architecture + how Redwood routes requests and batching example (show latency/cost chart)
3) 1:10–2:30 — Live demo: cold start to 50-token response on a private deployment (highlight KV cache hit)
4) 2:30–3:10 — Slide 6: cost/throughput summary + customer quote
5) 3:10–3:30 — Q&A prompt / next-steps CTA

Signature:
Hannah Schmitt
Sr. Field Marketing Manager, Redwood Inference
hannah@redwood.com | (650) 555-0199

Thanks Hannah — I converted the sketch into a script and attached a slide pass with time anchors. A few quick questions before I finalize:

- Audience level: is the room mostly engineering (ops/infra) or product/management? want to tune the demo depth accordingly.
- Demo environment: do you prefer we demo Hosted API or a Private deployment? I can show both at a high level but only one live demo is feasible in the slot.
- Tech check: Amy, can you confirm whether we’ll have a dedicated speaker laptop or need to bring our own device with HDMI adaptor?

Attached: [attachment: Redwood_demo_script_v1.md (MD)] [attachment: Redwood_CloudConf_slides_v3.pptx (PPTX) — draft slides with speaker notes]

Quick excerpt from the script:
"00:00 — 00:20: Hello, I’m Dev Patel, Product Engineer at Redwood. Today we’ll show a private deployment flow with a live 50-token generation and KV cache hit that speeds up repeat prompts by ~40%.
00:20 — 01:10: Slide 2: architecture (call out smart routing and quantization profiles).
01:10 — 02:30: Live demo: step through init, prefix cache usage, and token stream.
02:30 — 03:10: Cost example: before/after with batching.
03:10 — 03:30: CTA + mention dedicated capacity options."

Dev Patel

Subject: Re: Scheduling Redwood demo across EU & APAC — please confirm explicit zones

Hi Elena —

Quick note: when you wrote \"3:00 PM your time\" I want to be crystal clear about which zone so we don't accidentally schedule at midnight for someone. Because of DST differences in May: PT (PDT) is UTC-7, ET (EDT) is UTC-4, and most of Europe is on CEST (UTC+2). IST (India Standard Time) is UTC+5:30 and does not observe DST.

If you meant 15:00 PT (our local Pacific time), that would be:
  • 15:00 PT (PDT, UTC-7) = 22:00 UTC = 00:00 CEST (next day) = 03:30 IST (next day) — likely too late for EU/APAC.
If you meant 15:00 CEST (your local), that would be:
  • 15:00 CEST (UTC+2) = 13:00 UTC = 06:00 PT (PDT) = 09:30 IST — early for PT, OK for EU and morning for IST.
If you meant 15:00 IST (Amit's local), that would be:
  • 15:00 IST (UTC+5:30) = 09:30 UTC = 02:30 PT (PDT) = 11:30 CEST — early for PT.

To make this painless, could you confirm which of the three I listed in my first note you meant, or simply reply with the zone abbreviation (PT / ET / CEST / IST / UTC) next to the time? I'm re-suggesting three clear options below so everyone can pick without ambiguity (times shown as PT / ET / CEST / IST):

  Option 1 — Wed May 13 09:00 PT / 12:00 ET / 18:00 CEST / 21:30 IST
  Option 2 — Wed May 13 11:00 PT / 14:00 ET / 20:00 CEST / 23:30 IST
  Option 3 — Wed May 13 13:00 PT / 16:00 ET / 22:00 CEST / 01:30 IST (next day)

Please let me know which option you intended (or propose an explicit local time + zone). Once you confirm we'll send a calendar invite that includes all local times and UTC in the description so calendar apps don't misrender during DST transitions.

Thanks — Rafael

Subject: Access controls discovery — next steps & evidence request

Hi Sara — thanks again for a helpful demo earlier today. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we stay aligned: 

1) Scope we discussed: SSO (SAML 2.0 / IdP-initiated + SP-initiated), SCIM provisioning (user + group sync), RBAC for admin/non-admin roles, and audit logs retention for 12+ months.
2) Compliance items you asked about: SOC 2 Type II report, HIPAA/BAA posture, and GDPR data handling.

Requests / next steps: 
- Could you confirm who on your side will be the security reviewer (name/email) and preferred deadline for evidence? You mentioned an internal review by next Thursday.
- I'm attaching a short vendor questionnaire template to collect the specific SSO/SCIM artifacts we need (mappings, metadata.xml, endpoints). If it's easier, we can also take a completed PDF of your questionnaire.
- Proposed internal owners: Monica (security ops) will own the SSO/SCIM technical Qs; Karthik will handle contract/terms follow-up. I'll coordinate timelines and provide Redwood artifacts.

Attachments: Redwood_VSQ_SSO_SCIM_template.xlsx (stub) — a one-sheet checklist with fields we typically need.

If that looks good, I'll schedule a 30m follow-up with your security reviewer for early next week.

Thanks,
Rafael
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
rafael.mendes@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0124

--

- SCIM: example /scim/v2 endpoint swagger, attribute mapping example, and sample provisioning logs (last 30 days).
- RBAC: admin role matrix, console screenshots showing RBAC role assignments, and audit log sample CSV (token-level actions masked).

Requested from HealthCore by 2026-05-06 (so we can validate quickly): 
- Completed vendor questionnaire (PDF) — you mentioned you'll send this.
- Preferred IdP contact and test IdP (Okta/Azure AD) details or a test SAML metadata file.
- Any mandatory contractual clauses (data residency, BAA language) we should pre-check.

Attachments: Redwood_VSQ_SSO_SCIM_template.xlsx (filled-in example).

I'll also set up a 30-minute technical sync for Tue 2026-05-04 at 10:00 AM PT with Monica and John — tentative invite to follow; please confirm availability.

Thanks,
Rafael



From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
To: Sara Lewis <sara.lewis@healthcore.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 03 May 2026 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Access controls discovery — next steps & evidence request

Hi Sara — Monica from Security Ops here. A couple of technical notes ahead of the sync (and I've attached a small SCIM mapping example to speed validation):

1) SCIM details: Redwood supports SCIM 2.0 for provisioning. We typically provision the following attributes: userName, externalId, name(givenName,familyName), emails[type=work], active, and groups membership. Group sync is optional but recommended for RBAC.

2) Typical sync cadence: we support near-real-time SCIM (push on change) and scheduled full-syncs (daily). Please let us know if you require a different cadence.

3) SAML: we support both IdP-initiated and SP-initiated flows. For test integrations we prefer a short validity cert for verification. We'll provide sample metadata and a steps checklist during the call.

4) Audit logs & retention: we retain token-level and admin-action logs for 12 months by default; retention can be extended under Dedicated/Private deployments. We'll provide a redacted CSV sample for your review.

Attachments: scim_mapping_example.json (stub) — shows attribute mapping and a group-to-role mapping example.

Looking forward to the sync.

Thanks,
Monica
Security Operations | Redwood Inference
monica.patel@redwood.ai

- Any mandatory contractual clauses (data residency, BAA language) we should pre-check.

Hi Sara — Monica from Security Ops here. A couple of technical notes ahead of the sync (and I've attached a small SCIM mapping example to speed validation):

1) SCIM details: Redwood supports SCIM 2.0 for provisioning. We typically provision the following attributes: userName, externalId, name(givenName,familyName), emails[type=work], active, and groups membership. Group sync is optional but recommended for RBAC.

2) Typical sync cadence: we support near-real-time SCIM (push on change) and scheduled full-syncs (daily). Please let us know if you require a different cadence.

3) SAML: we support both IdP-initiated and SP-initiated flows. For test integrations we prefer a short validity cert for verification. We'll provide sample metadata and a steps checklist during the call.

4) Audit logs & retention: we retain token-level and admin-action logs for 12 months by default; retention can be extended under Dedicated/Private deployments. We'll provide a redacted CSV sample for your review.

title: P95 tail latency containment for model v8q — rollback authorization request
preview:
From: Anita Singh <anita@skyward.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 22 Apr 2026 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: High P95 for model v8q impacting production inference

Hi Kimberly / Priyom,

We started seeing a sustained P95 increase on requests routed to model v8q around 08:50 PDT this morning. Tail latency has jumped from ~420ms to ~1.2s and our user-facing timeout rate climbed as a result. This is affecting batch and single-shot flows.

Attached are a small set of traces and the live SLO dashboard (screenshot). Can you confirm whether v8q had a rollout or config change in the last 24 hours? If possible, we need either an immediate mitigation (variant fallback / rollback) or an ETA for a fix.

We are on a tight SLA window for the next two hours and would like to avoid customer-visible errors.

Thanks,
Anita Singh
Head of Platform Engineering
Skyward AI

Attachments: p95-traces-20260422.zip, sla-dashboard-snapshot.png

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>
To: Anita Singh <anita@skyward.ai>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 22 Apr 2026 09:28:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: High P95 for model v8q impacting production inference

Thanks for the alert, Anita — we see the same spike on our side.

Quick context: v8q was promoted to canary -> prod yesterday as part of the v8 family rollout. No config changes since the promotion. Priyom, Rafael — can you triage and propose a containment action?

Options to consider:
- Immediate rollback to the v8p variant that was stable last week
- Route affected customers to a lower-latency quantized variant (if quality acceptable)
- Apply conservative batching limits for this tenant to reduce queueing impacts

Anita — we will treat this as an SLA escalation. I am tentatively authorizing a rollback if the engineering lead recommends it, but need the runbook and estimated customer impact before proceeding. Rafael / Priyom: please attach the rollback runbook and perf diff.

Kimberly Park
Sr. Manager, Customer Reliability
Redwood Inference

Attachments: (none yet)

From: Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.ai>
Cc: Anita Singh <anita@skyward.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 22 Apr 2026 09:44:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: High P95 for model v8q impacting production inference

Digested the traces. Short summary:
- Regression appears to be in the KV-cache path for long-context requests; cache hit amplification for certain prompt patterns causes serialization on the GPU and amplifies tail latency.
- v8p (previous variant) uses a slightly different KV layout and does not show the same behavior in our repros.

Immediate recommendation:
1) Execute a safe rollback from v8q -> v8p for Skyward’s prod pool only (no global rollback) — expected P95 to return close to baseline within 5–7 minutes after cutover.
2) If rollback is not acceptable, route Skyward to v8p as a variant fallback for the next 4 hours.
3) Post-mortem and perf-diff attached (perf-diff-v8q-v8p.pdf).

Risks & notes:
- Rolling back will require brief routing churn and may cause a small number of duplicate-inference receipts (we’ll dedupe).
- We will place a temporary rate cap (30% of previous burst) during cutover to limit transient queueing.

I’ve prepared a rollback runbook (rollback-runbook.md) and can run on your authorization. ETA for rollback: ~20 minutes to prepare and ~10 minutes to complete.

Priyom Das
Principal Software Engineer — Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference

Attachments: perf-diff-v8q-v8p.pdf, rollback-runbook.md

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Anita Singh <anita@skyward.ai>
Date: Wed, 22 Apr 2026 10:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: High P95 for model v8q impacting production inference

Kimberly — from CS side, we recommend proceeding with the targeted rollback for Skyward. Key customer-facing notes for communications and SLA: 
- Requesting temporary SLA exception window for this incident (we propose 1 hour starting at rollback start) to avoid automatic crediting while we stabilize routing. See attached sla-exception-request.pdf for the formal ask.
- We should notify billing/sales to hold automated credit adjustments until we confirm tail latency returns to baseline.

If approved, I will:
- Send an update to Skyward ops on the rollback schedule and expected customer impact
- Open a HubSpot incident record and attach post-cutover telemetry

Rafael Mendes
Senior Customer Success Manager
Redwood Inference

Attachments: sla-exception-request.pdf

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Anita Singh <anita@skyward.ai>
Date: Wed, 22 Apr 2026 10:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: High P95 for model v8q impacting production inference

Authorization: APPROVED (targeted rollback for Skyward only).

Conditions:
- Limit rollback to the Skyward prod pool between 10:30–10:50 PDT.
- Apply 30% burst cap during the first 10 minutes post-cutover.
- Rafael: notify billing and Sales (hold auto-credit).
- Priyom: run the rollback runbook and confirm telemetry at 10-minute intervals; escalate to me immediately if P95 has not decreased by 50% within 15 minutes.

I am authorizing a 1-hour SLA exception window for this incident to prevent automatic credits; Legal will be looped if we need longer. Laura — please stand by to coordinate Customer Success comms.

Kimberly Park
Sr. Manager, Customer Reliability
Redwood Inference

Attachments: (none)

From: Anita Singh <anita@skyward.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 22 Apr 2026 12:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: High P95 for model v8q impacting production inference

Thank you — we appreciate the quick response and the targeted approach. Please confirm when rollback begins and when Skyward will be out of the exception window. We will pause any alerting on our side for the 10:30–10:50 window.

Anita
Anita Singh
Head of Platform Engineering
Skyward AI

Attachments: (none)

Hi Jordan,

- Wed 2026-04-14 10:00–10:30 PT
- Thu 2026-04-15 13:00–13:30 PT
- Fri 2026-04-16 09:00–09:30 PT

Date: 2026-04-09T14:40:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Intro: Redwood Inference demo — availability

Rafael —

Thanks for the options and for the materials. Quick update from our side: the executive team reprioritized a number of projects this quarter and the LLM/inference work has been pushed behind a couple of higher-priority initiatives (warehouse control system and fleet routing MVP). On top of that, a few of the folks who would evaluate your demo (including our Head of ML) are traveling for the next two weeks.

Given that, we need to pause active demos for Q2. I don’t want to string you along — can we postpone the demo and (for now) keep this on our radar with a touchpoint in early July? If you have a 1-page ROI summary that highlights expected token cost reductions and latency improvements for at-scale deployments, please send it over. Also copy procurement — they’ve started a pause on new vendor evaluations until headcount approvals land.

Appreciate the transparency.
Jordan

jordan.lee@greenharbor.com

Either way works — I’ll queue the materials to you and procurement. If it helps, I can surface a compact checklist for what a pilot would look like (estimated cost, timeline, minimal infra needs).

Rafael

preview:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Bradley Chen <brad.chen@olivetech.com>, Lina Gomez <lina.gomez@olivetech.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 8 Apr 2026 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Post-call recap: security checklist & next steps

Hi Bradley / Lina —

Thanks again for taking time today. Quick recap from our discovery call and the concrete next steps so we’re aligned: 

1) Current asks we heard from Olive Tech:
   - SOC 2 Type II report (most recent) and evidence of scope
   - Confirmation on GDPR / HIPAA coverage and whether Redwood will sign a BAA
   - Vendor security questionnaire completed (you shared it during the call)
   - Data residency / region options (US East requirement called out)

2) What Redwood will provide this week:
   - SOC 2 summary and attestation (redacted) — attached (Redwood_SOC2_Summary_2025.pdf)
   - Completed vendor questionnaire (attached as workbook template) — we’ll fill and return
   - Compliance roadmap doc outlining ISO 27001 timing and FedRAMP scoping — attached (Redwood_Compliance_Roadmap_2026.pdf)

Subject: MAP: Next steps and evaluation checkpoints

Laura / Procure team —

Thanks again for the time yesterday. Per our conversation I put together the first-cut MAP and evaluation checkpoints so we can align on the remaining gating items before contract signature. High-level asks:

- Security review (SOC2 evidence) — Novatech to confirm date for completion (target: 2026-04-20)
- SOW signoff and procurement PO (target: 2026-04-30)
- Pilot evaluation (3 week window) — proposed 2026-05-03 to 2026-05-23

I attached an initial SOW draft for reference (SOW_draft_v1). If any internal stakeholders need different timelines for procurement, let us know and we can propose a re-baselined MAP.

Small ask for the Novatech side: can you confirm who owns the security artifacts and whether a third-party vendor will be used for the SOC2 attestation? That will affect our internal readiness and the pilot start date.

Thanks — Rafael

Rafael Mendes
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
rafael.mendes@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0192

Cc: Novatech Procurement <procurement@novatech.io>, Ben Walters <ben.walters@novatech.io>
Date: 2026-04-07T11:30:00-07:00
Subject: Re: MAP: Next steps and evaluation checkpoints

Rafael / Marissa —

Thanks for pulling this together. Quick update from our side: the security team requested additional detail around data residency and audit log retention for the pilot. We can provide the SOC2 summary but expect their formal review to run into late April. Procurement also flagged a quarterly procurement freeze between Apr 20–May 5 that may delay PO issuance.

To be transparent: given the security review timing and the freeze, our current internal expectation is that the signature/PO could slip to mid-May. I attached the SOW draft you sent with a few inline comments (SOW_draft_v1.pdf).

Let us know if Novatech can start a limited evaluation without a PO (e.g., proof-of-concept under an MSA) or if you need the PO to provision dedicated capacity.

Regards,
Laura
Director, IT Security — Novatech

Attachment: Novatech_SOW_draft_v1.pdf (annotated)


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>, Sergio Alvarez <sergio.alvarez@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-04-09T08:05:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: MAP: Next steps and evaluation checkpoints

Forwarding Laura's note — heads up on two risk items that should go in the formal risk log:

1) Security review extension — impact: pilot start delayed by 2–3 weeks if SOC artifacts come late. Mitigation: provide a targeted evidence packet (data residency + retention policy + sample logs) and offer a live walk-through with security engineers to accelerate acceptance.

2) Procurement freeze window — impact: PO push into mid-May. Mitigation: propose a short-term MSA+letter-of-intent to start the pilot, or offer limited hosted credit for the pilot to begin prior to PO.

Rafael — I've drafted entries in the attached CSV (novatech_risk_log_20260412.csv) and an updated MAP showing two timeline scenarios (MAP_Novatech_v2.xlsx): baseline (PO by Apr 30) and re-baselined (PO by May 15). Could you review and decide which scenario to present to the Novatech execs? Also recommend we escalate to Sales VP if we have to commit credits or change discount terms.

Marissa
Senior Sales Manager, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai

Attachment: novatech_risk_log_20260412.csv; MAP_Novatech_v2.xlsx


From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Chen <laura.chen@novatech.io>, procurement@novatech.io, Ben Walters <ben.walters@novatech.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sergio Alvarez <sergio.alvarez@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-04-12T16:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: MAP: timeline reset & escalation — Novatech evaluation

Laura / Procurement team —

Appreciate the heads-up and the annotated SOW. Per Marissa's risk log and our internal review, we propose the following re-baselined MAP and an escalation path to avoid losing momentum:

Re-baselined dates (scenario B):
- Security acceptance artifacts due: 2026-04-27
- PO / signed SOW target: 2026-05-15
- Pilot window: 2026-05-18 to 2026-06-07

Planned mitigations we will execute immediately:
- Rafael: deliver a tailored evidence packet and schedule a live security walkthrough (proposed slot week of Apr 20).
- Marissa: prepare short-term pilot credit offer (up to $7,500) and a lightweight MSA template for procurement to review.
- Sergio: confirm capacity availability for a pilot starting May 18 and document costs for managed burst.

Escalation ask: if Novatech requires an earlier PO to secure quota for Q2, please indicate by Apr 16 so we can engage our sales leadership and finance for approval of credits or alternate contract terms. If we don't hear back by Apr 16, we'll proceed with Scenario B and reserve pilot capacity for the May start.

Attached: novatech_risk_log_20260412.csv (updated), MAP_Novatech_v2.xlsx, and link to DocuSign for the MSA draft: https://docusign.com/rooms/ABCD-1234

If helpful, Vivek can join a brief executive alignment call next week to unblock procurement.

Thanks — Rafael

Subject: Post-call recap: vector index fit + evaluation plan

Hi Claire — thanks again for the call earlier. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we stay aligned while you validate model fit on your side.

Call highlights (very short):
- Use case: product search + recommendations for knowledge-base + small catalog (~250k docs).
- Main success metrics you care about: recall@10 (human judged), MRR, and reduction in stale redirects (customer complaints).
- Current stack: in-house MiniLM embeddings (384d). You observed some topic drift and poor separation on short queries.

Preliminary Redwood recommendations (opinionated):
1) Evaluate two candidate embedding families: 
   - rw-embed-v1 (1536-d, Redwood-curated, optimized for semantic similarity)
   - sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L12-v2 (384-d, fast baseline)
2) Use a 1k-query benchmark set (mix of short navigational queries and long multi-sentence intents). We'll run recall@10, MRR, and embedding cosine distance distributions.
3) Dimensionality check: compare 384d vs 1536d. Higher-d may improve separation but costs storage/search latency. We'll include a PCA explained-variance curve in the report.
4) Drift signal: baseline drift test by month-over-month centroid shift and fraction of outlier queries (>3 std dev).

Attached: a starter prompt set (prompt_set_v2.txt), sample queries (sample_queries.csv) and a template QC workbook (benchmark_results_qc.xlsx). I ran a quick local pass with rw-embed-v1 on a 50k sample — see 'Quick notes' tab in the workbook.

- Claire: confirm access to a 1k production-ish query sample or let us pull anonymized logs (we can provide sanitization steps). Target: by Apr 14.

- Rafael: prepare a short A/B plan for index rollout and cost delta estimates.

Rafael / team — thanks for the quick reply and for confirming DocuSign as preferred. Attached is our subprocessor questionnaire; we've completed the high-level fields and included a short list of controls we expect for any subprocessors that will store EU personal data.

A couple items we need Redwood to confirm:
- Subprocessor eligibility: will Redwood allow subprocessors to be added dynamically per your standard clause, or do you require customer approval for additions?
- Backup / export controls: confirm whether backups are encrypted with customer-segregated keys (or Redwood-managed keys) and where backups are located (region).
- SCCs / transfer mechanism: ClearNet prefers execution of SCCs where EU data is involved — can you confirm you will sign the modular SCC (2021) if required?

Date: Tue, 7 Apr 2026 16:38:00 -0700
Subject: Re: DPA intake and subprocessor list for ClearNet

Maya — appreciate the detail. Short answers below and next steps for execution: 

- Subprocessor additions: our standard process is dynamic notification + 30-day objection window (customers can object to new subprocessors on reasonable grounds). We will provide a current attestation of processors used for ClearNet and commit to notifying you before adding any processor that will handle EU personal data.

- Backups / keys: backups are encrypted at rest. For backups that contain customer personal data we use Redwood-managed keys by default; we can discuss customer-managed keys for enterprise contracts (this is a procurement add-on). Backups are retained in the region(s) where instances are deployed — currently our US deployments replicate to us-west-2 and us-east-1.

- SCCs: yes, Redwood signs the EC Commission’s modular SCCs (2021) when EU personal data is in scope; legal will append SCCs as an exhibit when necessary.

Next steps: 
1) Laura will produce a 1-page attestation listing current subprocessors relevant to ClearNet and confirm which ones will see EU personal data.
2) I will send the DPA via DocuSign and route to Redwood Legal for signature. If ClearNet needs Adobe Sign please reply and we'll switch the execution path.

Laura / Olga — can you target EOD Thu for the attestation? Maya / Bob — once you review the attestation we'll expect any redlines to the DPA within 5 business days to hit the 3-week deadline.

Thanks,
Rafael Mendes
Enterprise Sales — Redwood Inference
rafael.mendes@redwoodai.com
+1 (415) 555-0134

--

title: Zenora - SLA remedies & latency exception (deal desk escalation)
preview:
From: Sanjay Kapoor <sanjay@zenora.ai>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.com>
Cc: Elise Tran <elise@zenora.ai>
Date: Wed, 1 Apr 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: SLA remedies + latency target for pilot

Hannah,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday — the team was impressed with the latency and the cost story. We are moving toward a pilot with two high-throughput routes and need to close on non-standard SLA terms before procurement signs off.

Specifically Zenora is requesting:
- Uptime target: 99.95% (monthly) instead of your standard 99.9%
- P95 request latency: 120ms (for the two routes listed in appendix)
- Service credits: up to 30% monthly credit for downtime beyond the target

We understand these are exceptions. Attached is our suggested amendment (Zenora_SLA_Amendment_v2.pdf) plus a small latency sample (latency-bench-zenora.csv). Can you confirm whether Redwood can accept the uptime bump and the higher credit cap? Also, what pricing or discount would you need to assume the extra risk?

Sanjay
CEO, Zenora
---
attachments: Zenora_SLA_Amendment_v2.pdf (application/pdf), latency-bench-zenora.csv (text/csv)

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 1 Apr 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Request: SLA remedies + latency target for pilot

Hannah / Marissa — forwarding Zenora’s ask. Quick summary from my side:
- Customer (mid-market AI infra) running two high-throughput endpoints (estimated 30M tokens/mo during pilot).
- Wants 99.95% uptime and P95 120ms; wants service credit up to 30% (higher than our standard 25% cap).
- They’re willing to sign a 12-month committed usage (we estimate $75k/mo committed), but they want the amended SLA and smaller fees on termination.

title: Fallback priority matrix & temporary RTO/RPO carveout request
preview:
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
To: Ellen Park <ellen@finraft.io>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 3 Apr 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Fallback priority matrix & temporary RTO/RPO carveout request

Ellen — thanks again for the time this morning. Per our call, I’m attaching a proposed fallback priority matrix and a short runbook extract that outlines the temporary RTO/RPO tolerances we can offer during the pilot cutover window (see attachments).

TL;DR: For the 6-week pilot we can offer a conditional RTO of 90 minutes and RPO of 30 minutes for the eu-west-2 standby region only, subject to the following: (1) warm-standby activation must be scheduled during a pre-agreed maintenance window; (2) we retain rights to route live traffic to the primary region first and to throttle non-critical batch jobs during activation; (3) FinRaft agrees to our proposed incident communication cadence.

Next steps I suggest:
- You review the attached matrix and highlight any resources you need prioritized (compute vs stateful storage).
- If the matrix looks OK, we’ll produce a short amendment to the SOW (one paragraph) and a DocuSign envelope to formalize the exception for the pilot.

I’ve looped in Monica and Karthik from our reliability and legal/ops teams for scope/approval. If you want a quick follow-up meeting to walk the matrix, I’ve got availability Monday and Wednesday morning PT.

Regards,
Rafael Mendes
Director, Customer Solutions
Redwood Inference

Attachments: proposed-fallback-matrix-v2.xlsx (spreadsheet), runbook-draft.pdf (pdf)


From: Ellen Park <ellen@finraft.io>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 3 Apr 2026 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Fallback priority matrix & temporary RTO/RPO carveout request

On Fri, Apr 3, 2026 at 9:12 AM Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com> wrote:
> Ellen — thanks again for the time this morning. Per our call, I’m attaching a proposed fallback priority matrix...

Thanks Rafael. Overall this aligns with what we discussed. A couple of clarifications from the FinRaft side before we can agree to the pilot carveout: 

1) Can you confirm whether the 90-minute RTO includes DNS propagation and health-check stabilization window? We need explicit wording because our runbooks count propagation separately.
2) For RPO=30m, does that assume prefix/KV cache persistence across regions (i.e., snapshot replication), or are we relying on rehydration from the primary?
3) We need an in-contract penalty cap for standby activation delays (we can accept a small credit if activation slips beyond agreed windows).

I’ll run this by our infra ops and legal and get back to you by EOD Monday. If you have a one-page amendment draft that calls out the activation window, propagation treatment, and credit cap, that would speed things up.

—Ellen
Ellen Park
VP, Infrastructure
FinRaft Analytics


From: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Ellen Park <ellen@finraft.io>
Date: Mon, 6 Apr 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: Fwd: Fallback priority matrix & temporary RTO/RPO carveout request

Hi all — quick answers to Ellen’s items and context for the amendment draft.

1) DNS propagation & stabilization: Our 90-minute RTO baseline does include the activation handoff but excludes external DNS TTL effects if FinRaft controls the external apex. If Redwood manages DNS for traffic cutover, we’ll include an explicit 15–45 minute propagation allowance in the RTO calculation; otherwise we’ll treat propagation as separate operational activity (and capture it in the runbook).

2) RPO assumptions: The 30m RPO in the matrix assumes continuous async replication of service-critical state (KV snapshots + event offsets). It does not assume immediate hot KV cache warmup; warmup is best-effort and falls under the activation checklist — we expect partial cache misses for ~10–20 minutes unless you adopt our prefix caching helper (we can instrument for you).

3) Credit cap: For pilot-level carveouts we typically offer a single one-time credit up to 2% of monthly committed fees if activation fails to meet the agreed SLA window. We can put that cap in the one-paragraph SOW amendment.

I’ve also attached a one-page amendment draft and a cleaned-up fallback-priority-matrix sheet that maps resource groups to activation priority. Rafael — if you agree, I’ll send the DocuSign envelope and include the one-time credit language. Ellen — please confirm you’re comfortable with the credit cap and the DNS handling preference so we can finalize.

Thanks,
Monica
Sr. Reliability Engineer, Redwood Inference

Attachments: proposed-fallback-matrix-v2.xlsx (spreadsheet), amendment-pilot-exception.docx (doc)

From: Ellen Park <ellen@finraft.io>
To: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 6 Apr 2026 15:40:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Fallback priority matrix & temporary RTO/RPO carveout request

Monica — that all sounds reasonable. A couple of small edits we’ll request to the amendment: make the DNS propagation clause explicit (we control external DNS) and add a line that Redwood will publish activation state to our incident channel within 10 minutes of switch. The 2% one-time credit is acceptable for the pilot.

Please send the updated one-paragraph amendment and DocuSign link and we’ll aim to sign within 48 hours pending a final infra check. Appreciate the fast turnaround.

Best,
Ellen
VP, Infrastructure — FinRaft Analytics

To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.ai>

Subject: RE: Fwd: Introduction: Azure private plan for ACME Health - next steps

Marissa / Rafael — reviewed Rafael's commercial and Stacey's asks. Attached are our redlines and a short summary below. I also pasted the key redline excerpts under the attachments section for quick scanning.

High-level: we can accept the private plan flow but need to negotiate a few clauses to limit corporate/legal exposure. Summary:
- SLA: ACME requests 99.95% — acceptable if we limit remedies to service credits only and cap credits at 25% of monthly fees in any 12-month period. Remove any automatic termination right for repeated breaches; add cure period.
- Indemnity: propose mutual indemnities for IP claims; limit Redwood's liability to the greater of $1,000,000 or 1x ARR (standard for enterprise).
- Data residency & SOC2: confirm Redwood will host ACME data in US East pools for production; provide SOC2 Type II report (we have the latest from 2025). Add a standard audit clause with reasonable notice and cost allocation (ACME pays third-party auditor unless agreed otherwise).
- Audit & logging: include clause for export of audit logs and an SLA for access (within 72 hours for security incidents).
- Export controls & regulated data: exclude processing of PHI unless a BAA is executed; if ACME requires PHI support, we need a separate addendum.

Attached: ACME_Redlines_Marissa_v3.pdf (legal redlines)

Key excerpt (from redlines):
> Section 4.2 (Service Levels): \"Service Credits shall be the sole and exclusive remedy for SLA failures. Redwood's aggregate liability for SLA-related credits shall not exceed 25% of fees paid by Customer in the preceding 12 months.\"

> Section 9.1 (Liability Cap): \"Except for willful misconduct and indemnity obligations, each party's aggregate liability shall be limited to the greater of (i) $1,000,000 and (ii) the fees paid by Customer to Redwood under this Agreement in the twelve (12) months preceding the claim.\"

If ACME confirms no PHI in scope, we can proceed without a BAA. If they have PHI, loop in Security and Product for feasibility and timeline impact.

Laura Bennett
General Counsel – Redwood Inference

Marissa / Rafael — reviewed Rafael's commercial and Stacey's asks. Attached are our redlines and a short summary below. I also pasted the key redline excerpts under the attachments section for quick scanning.

High-level: we can accept the private plan flow but need to negotiate a few clauses to limit corporate/legal exposure. Summary:
- SLA: ACME requests 99.95% — acceptable if we limit remedies to service credits only and cap credits at 25% of monthly fees in any 12-month period. Remove any automatic termination right for repeated breaches; add cure period.
- Indemnity: propose mutual indemnities for IP claims; limit Redwood's liability to the greater of $1,000,000 or 1x ARR (standard for enterprise).
- Data residency & SOC2: confirm Redwood will host ACME data in US East pools for production; provide SOC2 Type II report (we have the latest from 2025). Add a standard audit clause with reasonable notice and cost allocation (ACME pays third-party auditor unless agreed otherwise).
- Audit & logging: include clause for export of audit logs and an SLA for access (within 72 hours for security incidents).
- Export controls & regulated data: exclude processing of PHI unless a BAA is executed; if ACME requires PHI support, we need a separate addendum.

Attached: ACME_Redlines_Marissa_v3.pdf (legal redlines)

Key excerpt (from redlines):
> Section 4.2 (Service Levels): \"Service Credits shall be the sole and exclusive remedy for SLA failures. Redwood's aggregate liability for SLA-related credits shall not exceed 25% of fees paid by Customer in the preceding 12 months.\"

> Section 9.1 (Liability Cap): \"Except for willful misconduct and indemnity obligations, each party's aggregate liability shall be limited to the greater of (i) $1,000,000 and (ii) the fees paid by Customer to Redwood under this Agreement in the twelve (12) months preceding the claim.\"

If ACME confirms no PHI in scope, we can proceed without a BAA. If they have PHI, loop in Security and Product for feasibility and timeline impact.

Laura Bennett
General Counsel – Redwood Inference

title: Post-demo: cost targets & 1M-token benchmarking for FinOptics
preview:
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.ai>
To: John Doe <john.doe@finoptics.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 27 Mar 2026 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Post-demo: cost targets & 1M-token benchmarking for FinOptics

Thanks again for taking the demo today, John — good conversation. I wanted to capture the key pricing and volume items so we have a clean mutual action plan. Appended below are the asks we discussed and a compact benchmark vs typical hosting options to align expectations.

Quick recap (from our call):
- Primary workload: user-facing summarization + 1:1 assistant flows (average seq len ~400 tokens).
- Expected steady-state: ~300–500M tokens / month (peak bursts higher).
- Target unit economics: stakeholder asked for target $/1M tokens to compare to current Anthropic/OpenAI bills.
- Procurement constraints: legal will need SOC 2 + standard DPA, and they prefer single-tenant or dedicated pools for PII-heavy routes.

Action items I need from you to put a concrete proposal together:
1) Confirm a realistic monthly token range (low / expected / high).
2) Share any hard budget band (monthly or annual) for inference costs.
3) Indicate willingness to evaluate a 30–60 day trial on dedicated capacity (we can offer telemetry and a burn-rate cap).

Baseline benchmarking (illustrative — will produce formal sheet):
- OpenAI (gpt-4o-like host): ~$20–$25 per 1M tokens for long-context text gen at scale (varies by contract).
- Anthropic (claude family): ~$18–$22 per 1M tokens for comparable generative workloads in publicly-cited ranges.
- Open-model hosted providers (varies widely): $6–$15 per 1M tokens depending on quantization, NVMe cache, and batching.

We have attached a compact cost model (redwood-cost-model-1m.xlsx) and a one-pager comparison (hosting-comparison-summary.pdf). If you can push back the token bands and a budget target, I’ll model three scenarios (conservative / baseline / aggressive) and surface a target $/1M token proposal that factors in caching, quantization, and SLA options.

Proposed next steps:
- You: share the low/typical/high monthly token estimates + any budget ceilings (reply to this thread).
- Us: return a 1-page economics summary with a recommended $/1M price and tradeoffs by EOD Monday.

Thanks — Rafael
Rafael Mendes
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
rafael@redwood.ai
Attachments: redwood-cost-model-1m.xlsx (spreadsheet), hosting-comparison-summary.pdf (summary)

From: John Doe <john.doe@finoptics.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 27 Mar 2026 16:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: cost targets & 1M-token benchmarking for FinOptics

Rafael — thanks for the clear recap. Quick answers from our side:
- Monthly token estimate (rough): low 200M / typical 400M / high 650M (these include generation + input tokens).
- Procurement/budget: current line item for LLM infra is roughly $140k / month but we’re trying to drive that down; core product team is hoping for something closer to $60–90k / month if quality holds.
- Target $/1M: leadership has asked us to see options under $15 per 1M tokens ideally; $10 per 1M would be compelling for them if latency/quality are the same.

I attached our usage estimate CSV that the engineering team exported from a sample week. Legal will require SOC 2 type II and a short security questionnaire — happy to loop procurement if you want.

We’re open to a 30–60 day dedicated trial but prefer a predictable committed rate for that period. Let us know how much runway you need to spin a dedicated pool for our traffic profile.

John
John Doe
Head of Platform Engineering | FinOptics Inc
john.doe@finoptics.com
Attachments: finoptics-usage-estimates.csv

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.ai>
To: John Doe <john.doe@finoptics.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 28 Mar 2026 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: cost targets & 1M-token benchmarking for FinOptics

Appreciate the numbers, John — very helpful. I ran a quick sensitivity with the inputs you provided and wanted to surface a pragmatic path:

Preliminary model highlights (high level):
- At ~400M tokens / month: Redwood hosted dedicated pool w/ prefix caching and INT8 quantization conduit could target ~$9–$11 per 1M tokens (assuming 99th-pct latency SLO of ~300ms for active routes).
- At 200M tokens / month: unit cost is higher (~$13–$16 per 1M) because fixed infra overhead is amortized across fewer tokens.
- At 650M tokens / month: we can push to ~$6–$8 per 1M with more aggressive batching and a modest committed capacity discount.

Notes on what moves price: caching (prefix and KV) and quantization are the biggest levers. If you can identify ~20% of tokens that are prefix-stable (repeated system prompts, canned responses), the effective $/1M can drop materially. Also, routing lower-priority requests to an open-model quantized variant reduces costs further but adds small delta in tail latency and occasional content style differences.

Proposed proposal for procurement clarity:
- Option A (Baseline): 3-month pilot, committed 400M tokens/month, price $10 / 1M tokens, standard DPA + SOC 2 evidence, telemetry export.
- Option B (Conservative): 1-month eval, no commit, variable billing ~ $13 / 1M tokens (higher to cover reserve).
- Option C (Scale): 12-month commit, 600M baseline, $7 / 1M tokens + burst capacity, SLA-backed latency.

Next steps I’ll take on Monday: produce the 1-pager economics (spreadsheet) and a short Appendix that maps our price to publicly-available OpenAI/Anthropic pricing points and an estimated annual spend for each scenario. CCing Marissa (AE) to coordinate procurement cadence and Connor (SE) for an isolated trial plan and perf verification steps.

If you can confirm which option you prefer or your preferred budget band, we’ll draft the procurement-friendly doc and timeline.

Cheers — Rafael
Rafael Mendes
Account Executive | Redwood Inference

From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.ai>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 28 Mar 2026 09:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: cost targets & 1M-token benchmarking for FinOptics

Quick note internal — sounds like FinOptics is price-sensitive and prefers a committed model if we can hit $8–10 / 1M. Rafael, please schedule the review deck for Monday EOD so I can loop procurement. Connor, can you draft the trial plan + perf checklist (latency targets, sample dataset access, SOC2 artifact list)? I’ll prepare the initial SOW template keyed to Option A and C so procurement has something to react to.

Thanks — Marissa
Marissa Cole
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference

title: Potential data exposure — coordinating notification timelines, subprocessors & cooperation
preview:
From: Alice Yuan <alice.yuan@purityhealth.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Bob Chen <bob.chen@vendorsecure.io>
Date: Sun, 22 Mar 2026 08:47:00 -0700
Subject: Potential data exposure — initial report and ask for notification timeline

Hi Karthik / Marissa,

We detected anomalous activity in one of our integrations with Redwood this morning. At a high level: a subset of records (user metadata, hashed IDs) appear to have been accessible via an expired temporary token from a subprocessor endpoint. Purity Health has started an initial containment step and we are collecting logs. We do not yet have indications of exfiltration but we need to move quickly.

Immediate asks:
- Can Redwood confirm whether this meets your DPA definition of a \"personal data breach\" or if this falls under an operational incident?
- If it is not a reportable breach, please confirm within 24 hours so we can communicate internals. If it is reportable, please provide an initial notification timeline (we typically need an initial statement within 72 hours to our regulators).
- Please share the subprocessor contact for VendorSecure (we've looped Bob above) so we can coordinate forensic access.
- Any customer-facing wording you recommend would be helpful.

Attached: prelim_event_summary.pdf (high-level timeline and screenshots).

We'd appreciate acknowledgement ASAP — ideally within one business day.

Thanks,
Alice
Alice Yuan
CISO, Purity Health
alice.yuan@purityhealth.com


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Alice Yuan <alice.yuan@purityhealth.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Bob Chen <bob.chen@vendorsecure.io>
Date: Sun, 22 Mar 2026 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Potential data exposure — initial report and ask for notification timeline

Alice — thanks for the heads up and for the summary doc. We acknowledge receipt and have opened an internal incident.

High-level plan / proposed timeline:
- 0–24h: Triage & containment (acknowledgement to you provided now).
- 24–72h: Initial notification to Purity Health with confirmed scope + any immediate mitigations.
- 5 business days: Detailed incident report with root cause analysis and remediation steps.

We will also:
- Provide a subprocessor contact matrix (attached: subprocessor_contact_list.csv).
- Share our incident-notification-playbook.docx so you have the template for customer-facing language.
- Schedule a 30-minute sync tomorrow 10:00 AM PT to walk through preliminary findings unless that time doesn't work for you.

Neha/Vivek — tagging you for legal/forensics input and to confirm the timeline is consistent with contract obligations (DPA/SCCs). Rafael/Marissa — please start drafting a customer update that meets the regulatory constraints (we'll keep customers informed per Purity's cadence).

I'll be on the call and will coordinate cross-functional responses.

Best,
Karthik Iyer
Head of Sales, Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.com


From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Alice Yuan <alice.yuan@purityhealth.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 22 Mar 2026 13:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Potential data exposure — initial report and ask for notification timeline

Thanks, Karthik and Alice. From a DPA / privacy standpoint:

1) Notification timing: our DPA uses the phrase "without undue delay" and aligns to "where feasible and appropriate, within 72 hours" for initial notification to the controller. Given Purity's regulator constraints, the 72-hour window is appropriate for an initial statement — we'll aim to get the scope + initial mitigations within that time.

2) Scope: initial notice should include (to the extent known):
- nature of the incident (systems and subprocessor endpoints affected)
- categories of personal data potentially involved (we should be conservative if uncertain)
- estimated number of records (range is fine for initial)
- contact for further information and remediation steps taken

3) Subprocessors & cooperation: Our DPA requires Redwood to notify customers of changes to subprocessors and to provide reasonable cooperation to enable the controller to meet its regulatory obligations. Suggested cooperation clause language (for internal use / quick reference):

\"Where Redwood becomes aware of a security incident that may affect Customer Data, Redwood will (a) notify the Customer without undue delay and, where feasible, within seventy-two (72) hours of becoming aware, (b) provide a description of the nature and scope of the incident, the categories of data affected, and the steps taken to mitigate, and (c) cooperate with the Customer and its designated representatives (including Customer's designated external forensic providers) to facilitate forensic investigation and regulatory obligations. Redwood will provide contact details for any relevant subprocessors and facilitate coordination where necessary.\"

4) Data transfers: If any of the affected processing involved cross-border transfers (e.g., EU <-> US subprocessors), include the legal basis and any SCC/transfer notes in the follow-up report.

I'll drop a short template for the 72-hour notice and we can iterate before sending.

-Neha
