title: Optimize toolkit packaging & discount — quantization + batching recommendations
preview:
From: Elena Ramos <elena@datawave.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@datawave.com
Date: 2026-03-18T09:15:00Z
Subject: Optimize toolkit — high-level ask and numbers

Hi Karthik,

Thanks for the intro last week. We're evaluating Redwood's Optimize toolkit to reduce token unit costs on our inference workloads. High level: 10M generated tokens/month, mostly chat assistant flows with ~150 token prompts and 250 token generations; peak concurrency ~60 reqs/s.

Two asks to start pricing:
- Can you provide packaging options for Optimize (pilot vs committed tiers) and an example discount schedule tied to committed spend?
- Your recommended quantization profiles and batching/caching guidance that would help us hit $0.0006/token (target) for production.

Attached is a quick usage CSV. We're hoping to finalize by end of month.

Thanks —
Elena
Director of Platform Engineering
DataWave Labs
[Attachment: datawave_usage_march.csv (csv)]

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Ramos <elena@datawave.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-03-19T10:12:00Z
Subject: Re: Optimize toolkit — high-level ask and numbers

Elena — thanks for the details, this is helpful. High level answers below; I'll follow up with a short pricing worksheet and a quantization PDF.

Packaging/discounts:
- Pilot (3 months): 20% off list for first 3 months, no commit. Good for validation.
- Committed tier: tiered discounts starting at 10% for <$80k/mo, 20% for $80-200k/mo, 30% >$200k/mo (annual commitment).
- We can offer a one-time migration credit to help with the trial if you sign a 6-month runway.

Quantization + performance guidance (short):
- Model family: use int8 quant profiles for generation-heavy flows; mixed-int8 for chat with long contexts.
- KV cache and prefix caching: enable prefix caching for assistant prompts (saves ~35% of token compute on repeated system prompts).
- Batching: continuous micro-batching with max latency budget 120ms will usually increase throughput 3-4x without user-visible latency for your workload.

I'm attaching a short PDF with recommended QProfiles and a pricing workbook. Can we do a 30-min call tomorrow to align on target SLOs and finalize the quote?

Thanks,
Karthik
Head of Enablement, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: quant-recs.pdf (pdf)]

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-03-20T08:22:00Z
Subject: Re: Optimize toolkit — pricing worksheet

Looping in pricing ops. Quick notes from our side after reviewing Elena's numbers and Karthik's draft:
- Margin sensitivity: deep quantization helps, but we shouldn't commit to >30% discount on first-year committed ARR unless we get >=12 month term + minimum throughput SLA.
- Pilot discount (20%) is fine but requires a strict termination window and no long-term SLA.
- Recommend we provide an attachment showing the worked pricing with assumed batching gains and token-cost per model variant.

Attaching the Excel with the worked scenarios. Rafael
Commercial Finance, Redwood
[Attachment: Optimize_Sizing_and_Pricing.xlsx (xlsx)]

From: Elena Ramos <elena@datawave.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-03-20T14:05:00Z
Subject: Re: Optimize toolkit — pricing worksheet

Karthik / Rafael — gratitude for the fast turnaround. A couple of clarifying asks before we take this to procurement: 
1) For the committed tier, can you pin which quantization profile you expect in the contract (e.g., mixed-int8 + per-token floor) so our auditors can validate cost claims?
2) For batching/caching, we'd like a recommended config that balances user latency (p95 < 350ms) while delivering the cost savings you modeled. Can you provide explicit examples (batch sizes, KV TTLs, cache hit expectations)?
3) Pricing ask: DataWave is prepared to commit to a 12-month $120k/mo spend if you can commit to the 20% tier at that level and include a 10% rollback protection credit if we miss production SLOs in any given month.

We can sign the pilot now, but we'd prefer the committed path if pricing and the SLO credit are ok.

Thanks,
Elena
Director of Platform Engineering
DataWave Labs
[Attachment: procurement_requirements.pdf (pdf)]

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Ramos <elena@datawave.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-03-21T17:03:00Z
Subject: Re: Optimize toolkit — pricing & mutual action plan

Elena — appreciate the clear asks. Below is a proposed mutual action plan and the explicit operational recommendations you requested. If acceptable, I'll ask Legal to include a short SLA credit clause and Pricing Ops to lock the 20% discount at $120k/mo for a 12-month commitment (deal DW-8791).

Mutual action plan (high level):
- Week 0: Sign 3-month pilot (20% off) to validate migration and get telemetry.
- Week 4-6: Validate batching + cache gains on a shadow workload.
- Week 8: Executive review — if observed cost reduction >= target (>=40% net token cost reduction vs baseline), sign 12-month commit at $120k/mo with 20% discount and a rollback credit clause.

Explicit configuration recommendations (for your engineers):
- Quantization profile: mixed-int8 for the LLMs used in generation, int8 for any encoder-only or short-context models. Use Redwood QProfile v2.1 (see attached PDF) with per-token fallbacks enabled for safety. Expected model perf delta: ~1-2% quality degradation, ~2.8x reduction in GPU token cost.
- Batching: continuous micro-batching with target batch sizes of 8-12 for 95th percentile concurrency, max aggregation latency 100ms. For request types with strict latency (user-facing single-turn chat), set max latency budget to 80-120ms and enable adaptive resizing.
- Caching: enable prefix caching for system and static instructions with TTL 24 hours; enable KV cache reuse for session windows up to 30 minutes. Cache hit expectation: 20-35% for your assistant flows based on supplied traces — this maps to ~12-18% token-cost reduction in our model.
- Observability: instrument per-route token counts and cache hit metrics; we'll provide a Redwood dashboard template.

On the rollback credit: we can agree to a monthly 10% credit against that month's charges if monthly p95 latency >350ms AND observed token-cost reduction <30% vs modeled projection, subject to mutual debugging window. Legal will need to review exact language; I'll kick that off once you confirm.

Attachments: mutual-action-plan.docx (draft M.A.P.), quant-recs.pdf (recommendation summary), Optimize_Sizing_and_Pricing.xlsx (scenarios).

Related hubspot: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/987654321
Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2B3c

If that looks good, we can schedule a short exec alignment with pricing ops and legal next week.

Best,
Karthik
Head of Enablement, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: mutual-action-plan.docx (docx)]

title: Re: SIG: Audit logging retention & customer access (follow-up)
preview:
From: Jared Cole <jared.cole@acmebank.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>; Acme Bank Security Team <security@acmebank.com>
Cc: 
Date: Tue, 10 Mar 2026 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: SIG questionnaire: audit logging - a few clarifications

Hi Monica,

Thanks for the SIG submission you sent over last week. Our security review has a handful of follow-ups on the audit logging section (SIG reference: SIG-LOGS-5.1 - "Event logging and retention"). Specifically:

- Can you confirm the event types that are captured (auth events, API calls, admin actions, model inference requests, data access, etc.)?
- What is your default retention period for hosted customers? Do customers have the ability to configure retention?
- Do audit logs include request and response bodies or only metadata? Any PII redaction/filters applied?
- How can customers access their logs (console, API, streaming to SIEM)? Any proofs or sample exports we can validate?

If you can provide a short statement and attach the log retention policy + a redacted sample export (CSV or JSONL), that would help move us to the next procurement step.

Appreciate it—happy to hop on a short call if easier.

Best,
Jared
Jared Cole
Senior InfoSec Manager, Acme Bank


From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
To: Jared Cole <jared.cole@acmebank.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 11 Mar 2026 09:18:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: SIG questionnaire: audit logging - a few clarifications

Hi Jared,

Thanks — great questions. Below are concise answers mapped to the SIG items you referenced; I attached our Audit Log Retention & Access Policy (v1.2) and a small redacted sample export (sample-audit-log-20260310.csv).

1) Event types captured:
- Authentication events (successful & failed logins, token grants).
- Authorization/admin actions (role changes, IAM updates, console admin actions).
- API requests (model selection, generation requests, function/tool calls).
- Data access events (file uploads/downloads, embeddings/lookup ops).
- System events (service restarts, infra autoscale events).
- Security events (failed auth, suspicious IPs, key usage anomalies).

2) Retention (hosted):
- Default retention: 365 days (one year) for primary audit logs.
- Tiering: 90 days stored hot for fast query; remainder moved to cold storage.
- Customers on Dedicated/Private can request custom retention windows (shorter or longer) via contract and configuration.
- Policy name: "Audit Log Retention & Access Policy v1.2" (attached).

3) Log contents & PII: 
- Logs are emitted in JSONL by default; sample attached in CSV form for review.
- We capture full request metadata (timestamp, request id, user id hash, API endpoint, model id, token counts, latency, status codes).
- We do NOT store customer prompt/response text in the standard audit stream by default. For model-inference events we record metadata and model output hash only.
- PII handling: we apply automated redaction for known PII fields in metadata and provide a configurable filter pipeline for customers who request additional redaction.

4) Customer access & exports:
- Console: customers can download per-job logs for a selected time range.
- API: REST endpoint for programmatic export (authenticated with customer API key).
- Streaming: enterprise customers can enable real-time forwarders (JSONL over HTTPS or SFTP) to send logs to SIEMs; see section 3.4 of the attached policy.
- Auditability: logs are write-once in our S3-backed immutable store with object versioning and WORM-configurable buckets for customers requiring tamper-evidence.

Attached: Audit Log Retention & Access Policy v1.2.pdf; sample-audit-log-20260310.csv (redacted).

If you need a signed statement for procurement, we can provide a short attestation on our practices and retention windows — Rafael is looped here to help with the contractual language.

Thanks,
Monica Patel
Senior Security & Compliance Manager
Redwood Inference
monica.patel@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0182

---

Hello Marcus,

This is a courtesy notice that your annual subscription for Cloudflare WAF + API Gateway (account 448-REDWOOD) expires on 2026-05-01. Based on current usage we have prepared a renewal proposal that includes:
- Core WAF coverage (enterprise ruleset)
- API Gateway throughput plan (monthly burst options)

To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 26 Feb 2026 10:08:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Renewal notice: Cloudflare WAF & API Gateway — term starting 2026-05-01

Marcus / team,

Quick infra readout after a brief audit:
- Avg daily API gateway egress tokens (Jan 2026): 1.2B tokens/day (see attached Edge_Traffic_Report_Jan2026.csv).
- Current committed burst capacity is underutilized (~18% utilization over last 3 months).
- We have a rate-based rule set active for 22 high-traffic endpoints — removing those would risk false negatives for production traffic.

Recommendation: pursue a blended pricing approach — keep enterprise WAF, reduce committed burst tier by one level, add token overage at negotiated step-pricing. This should save ~12–15% on list pricing while preserving rules coverage.

Ping me for the call slot tomorrow at 11:00 PT.

-Vivek
Senior Infra Engineer

[Attachment: Edge_Traffic_Report_Jan2026.csv (csv)]

From: Tara Singh <tara.singh@cloudflare.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Cc: contracts@cloudflare.com, procure@procureco.com
Date: Fri, 27 Feb 2026 14:22:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Renewal options & draft SOW for Redwood Inference

Hi Marcus / Monica,

Thanks for the quick response and the usage data Vivek shared — helpful. Based on your utilization profile our proposal options are: 
A) Full enterprise renewal (status quo) — 3% annual uplift, includes premium ruleset and multi-region failover.
B) Blended plan — reduce committed burst by one tier, add step-pricing for overage, 10% discount on list (savings estimate matches Vivek's estimate).
C) Two-year prepaid — 14% discount if you commit to 24 months and one-time upfront payment (requires legal approval).

I attached an updated rates workbook with these options and a short SOW addendum that clarifies SLA crediting, data residency, and audit windows. We can also add a clause for a 60-day pricing hold while redlines are resolved — I can route that through our contracts team.

Would you like us to prepare a redline-friendly Word SOW for your legal team?

Cheers,
Tara

title: Post-call: Streaming UX + model-fit follow-up and POC next steps
preview:
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@stromax.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-03-08T11:15:00-07:00
Subject: Post-call recap + recommended models for streaming POC

Hi Liam — great to meet earlier. Short recap and proposed next steps from our discussion: 

Summary of what we heard
- Primary goal: embed a low-latency streaming assistant into the Stromax web console for step-by-step guidance (docs + code snippets).
- Quality bar: human-like coherence for mid-length responses (typical outputs ~120–400 tokens) with minimal mid-stream contradictions.
- Latency target: first-token <= 300ms for 32–64 token bursts; tail token throughput acceptable up to 20 tokens/sec.
- Risk areas you flagged: partial output coherence (mid-stream hallucination), stop-sequence misfires in multi-turn flows, and occasional latency spikes during concurrency peaks.

Model & config recommendations (initial)
- Model family: try Mistral-Instruct-7B (instruct-tuned) and Llama-2-13B-chat variants for baseline quality; both have good streaming characteristics in our catalog.
- Quantized variant: mistral-7b-q4f / llama-13b-q4 for Dedicated/Private if you want to drop per-token cost without large quality regressions.
- Streaming knobs to try in the POC: stream_chunk_tokens=8–16, early_flush=false, max_new_tokens=512, stop_sequences configured explicitly per flow (see attached prompt_set_v1.json).
- Cache: enable prefix KV caching for templated prompts to reduce cost and jitter on repeated flows.

Benchmarks + prompt sets
- Attached: prompt_set_v1.json (sample prompts and stop-sequence examples).
- Benchmark snapshot: https://redwood-internal.s3.amazonaws.com/benchmarks/streaming/2026-03-08/index.html — contains throughput/latency sweeps and partial-coherence scores for the models above.

Proposed POC and next steps
1) Technical POC (3 business days): Redwood runs a streaming bench against 3 representative prompts you provide + our standard prompt set. Goal: measure first-byte latency distribution, streaming chunk coherence, and stop-sequence reliability. Rafael will coordinate infra.
2) Shared eval: we’ll drop the bench artifacts and a short eval report to the shared Drive.
3) Follow-up call to review results and agree on Dedicated vs Hosted routing policy.

If that sounds good, can you share 6–8 high-priority prompts and a sample of your production concurrency (avg sessions/min)? I attached a short example transcript from a previous POC that highlights the "partial output coherence" case we discussed.

Thanks —
Marissa Cole
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0192

Attachment: prompt_set_v1.json (application/json), stream_bench_sample.txt (text/plain)

From: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@stromax.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Stromax Product Team <product@stromax.com>
Date: 2026-03-08T13:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + recommended models for streaming POC

Marissa — thanks, this is helpful. A few clarifying points from our side: 

1) Example failure modes: we see the assistant begin an instruction set then mid-output repeat itself or switch topics — appears tied to chunking. I attached a short snippet under "stream_bench_sample.txt" showing a live session where a stop token appears but the model continues.

2) Stop sequences: our UI relies on two stop markers — a hard stop (

) and a soft stop ("### END"). When soft stop is hit mid-stream we often still get trailing tokens. Is this due to partial buffer flushing or mismatch between model-side stop filtering and client-side truncation?

3) Latency spikes: peak concurrency is ~120 concurrent sessions during demos, average session requests are ~1.7 req/s. We can provision Dedicated capacity if that's the recommendation, but want to validate behavior under streaming first.

Can the POC exercise both: (a) hosted streaming with your smart-routing fallback to a smaller model, and (b) Dedicated quantized variant, so we can compare quality vs cost under the same prompt set?

Liam O'Connor
Lead Product Manager, Stromax
liam.oconnor@stromax.com | +44 20 7946 0912

Attachment: stream_bench_sample.txt (text/plain)

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@stromax.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-03-09T09:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + recommended models for streaming POC

Appreciate the specifics, Liam — great examples. A few concrete answers and proposed tuning to address each of your items: 

Partial output coherence / chunking
- Root cause: most often it's a mismatch between stream_chunk_tokens and the model's internal lookahead; setting chunk size too small (e.g., 1–4 tokens) increases chance of mid-sentence state loss. For your flows we recommend stream_chunk_tokens=8–16 and stream_flush_interval=80–120ms. That keeps chunks big enough to maintain coherence while still giving low perceived latency.

Stop sequences
- Two-tier approach we recommend: server-side stop filter (model inference stage) + client-side truncation as a safety net. Server-side handles canonical stops; client-side enforces UI markers so trailing tokens are clipped before render. In practice we add an explicit finalizing token in the prompt ("<END_OF_RESPONSE>") and map that to your soft stop "### END" client-side to reduce ambiguity. Sample changes are in prompt_set_v2.json (attached).

Latency spikes
- If spikes correlate with KV cache misses, enabling prefix caching for templated prompts lowers variance substantially. For concurrency 120 sessions, Dedicated with 4x A100-equivalent slots (or our dedicated medium pool) is a reasonable starting point. For hosted we can set smart-routing: route to larger model for edge cases and fall back to quantized variant when latency > 450ms.

POC plan (updated)
- 3-day bench that runs both hosted (smart-routing enabled) and a Dedicated quantized variant. Day 1: baseline sweeps (no caching). Day 2: enable prefix cache + adjust stream_chunk_tokens. Day 3: production-sim (concurrency profile you provided). Deliverable: latency percentile table, streaming-coherence score (automated), stop-sequence failure rate, and the artifacts (logs + transcripts).

Action items
- Liam: share 6–8 representative prompts and a 30-minute sample traffic profile (start/end times and concurrent sessions).
- Rafael: can you own the Dedicated slot request and set up the bench infra?

I attached prompt_set_v2.json with the proposed finalization token and stop mapping; that should reduce trailing-token cases in most flows. Happy to hop on a 30-min technical sync with your infra folks before we start.

—
Marissa Cole
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0192

Attachments: prompt_set_v2.json (application/json)

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@stromax.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-03-10T09:37:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + recommended models for streaming POC

Thanks — Rafael here. I'll take point on the Dedicated trial provisioning and coordinate the bench windows with Stromax. A few logistics items so we can size correctly: 

1) Throughput targets: confirm expected peak concurrency (you mentioned ~120). Do you have a p95 request size (tokens) or average reply length? That helps pick GPU pool size.
2) SLO ask: we can target first-token p50 < 200ms and p95 < 350ms for the quantized Dedicated pool with the chunking settings Marissa suggested; hosting will likely show larger variance.

Proposal: I’ll provision 4 GPU slots (A100-equivalent), install the quantized runtime, and run the three-day POC. If we see memory pressure on Day 2 we can scale to 6 slots. Rafael will also upload the bench artifacts to the HubSpot deal and Drive once complete.

Rafael Mendes
Solutions Engineering (Dedicated Trials) | Redwood Inference
rafael.mendes@redwood.ai | +1 (650) 555-0147

Related link: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/4821

title: Request for Net60 & Credit Limit — Atlas Finance (negotiation)
preview:
From: Sofia Alvarez <sofia@atlasfinance.eu>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marcus Lindström <marcus.l@atlasfinance.eu>
Date: Thu, 5 Mar 2026 09:12:00 +0100
Subject: Request: Net60 + increased credit line for Q2 invoicing

Hi Marissa,

Hope you’re well — quick one from procurement at Atlas. Our standard AP payment cadence is Net60 and we need vendor terms to align for a multi-quarter engagement starting April. We’re planning to ramp usage quickly and would like to request: 

- Net60 payment terms (company-wide)
- A credit limit of EUR 200,000 to cover estimated monthly run-rate during onboarding
- Monthly consolidated invoicing (end of month)

On VAT: we operate in Sweden and will be using the reverse-charge mechanism for B2B services. I’ve attached our VAT certificate to speed tax handling.

Can you confirm process and what you need from our side (trade refs, credit app, PO, etc.)? We’d like a decision before our finance cycle on 15 Mar.

Thanks —
Sofia Alvarez
Head of Procurement, Atlas Finance
sofia@atlasfinance.eu
Attachment: VAT_certificate.pdf (PDF, 120KB)


From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sofia Alvarez <sofia@atlasfinance.eu>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 5 Mar 2026 11:00:00 +01:00
Subject: Re: Request: Net60 + increased credit line for Q2 invoicing

Hi Sofia — thanks for the note and the VAT cert. Welcome — excited to have Atlas moving to production with Redwood.

Short answer: we can start the credit review now, but our standard commercial terms are Net30 with a baseline credit review. For Net60 and a EUR 200k limit we’ll need a completed credit application and trade references, plus internal finance sign-off. I’ve looped in Rafael (Finance) and Ben (Sales Ops) to run the review and propose options.

Immediate next steps we need from Atlas to scope a proposal:
- Completed credit_application.pdf (I can send our template)
- Purchase order for the first month (PO helps expedite)
- Confirmation of the VAT ID (we received the cert, thank you)

If timing is tight, we can offer a provisional arrangement while the credit check completes — Rafael will outline what that looks like.

I’ll follow up once Rafael confirms the provisional terms and timeline.

Best,
Marissa Cole
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
marissa@redwoodinference.com


From: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sofia Alvarez <sofia@atlasfinance.eu>
Date: Fri, 6 Mar 2026 08:45:00 +01:00
Subject: RE: Request: Net60 + increased credit line for Q2 invoicing

Marissa / Ben / Sofia — looping in Finance to propose a pragmatic path.

Quick overview from finance: Redwood typically grants Net30 for new customers; credit limits are set after a review. For enterprise requests we can either: 

Option A — Full credit review (standard):
- Receive completed credit_application.pdf (attached template)
- Two trade references and last 3 months of topline/POs if available
- Typical decision window: 5–7 business days
- Possible credit limit: up to EUR 150k on initial approval

Option B — Provisional commit (temporary while review completes):
- We can approve a provisional credit line of EUR 100k for 30 days to allow onboarding (Net45), conditional on signed credit app and a PO for the first invoice
- If credit review completes positively before 30 days, we’ll increase to negotiated limit and move to Net60 if approved

Notes on VAT: as long as Atlas provides a valid VAT ID and evidence (VAT_certificate.pdf), we can apply reverse-charge treatment on EU invoices. Finance will require the VAT ID in the credit application and the PO to carry the VAT number.

Attachments: credit_application.pdf (fillable PDF) — please complete and return.

Proposed recommendation: offer Net45 provisional terms with EUR 150k provisional limit while the full review is underway. Net60 would require stronger credit references or an increased initial deposit/prepayment for the first one or two invoices.

Happy to discuss on a quick call if that helps move the decision before your 15 Mar cycle.

Regards,
Rafael Mendes
Director, Finance Operations | Redwood Inference
rafael@redwoodinference.com
Attachment: credit_application.pdf (fillable PDF)


From: Sofia Alvarez <sofia@atlasfinance.eu>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, 7 Mar 2026 14:30:00 +01:00
Subject: Re: Request: Net60 + increased credit line for Q2 invoicing

Thanks Rafael — appreciate the clarity. A few points from our end: 

- Atlas has long payment cycles with our treasury; Net60 is standard. Net45 would still put pressure on cash conversion for us given the size of the engagement.
- We can provide trade references (I’ll attach two) and the PO, but providing additional deposit for the first two invoices is a non-starter under our procurement policy.
- VAT: confirmed — our VAT ID is SE556677889901, attached. Reverse charge is applied on our side.

Given procurement constraints, our preference is: Net60 with EUR 200k limit. If Redwood cannot commit to Net60 immediately, Atlas would be willing to proceed with a signed multi-month PO and milestone-based invoice schedule (end-of-month invoicing) but needs the full 60-day payment window.

Attachments: trade_reference_1.pdf, trade_reference_2.pdf, VAT_certificate.pdf (previously sent)

Regards,
Sofia


From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sofia Alvarez <sofia@atlasfinance.eu>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 8 Mar 2026 10:15:00 +01:00
Subject: Re: Request: Net60 + increased credit line for Q2 invoicing

Sofia — thanks for the additional docs and for being candid on procurement constraints. I ran Rafael’s note and your constraints past leadership. Here’s a pragmatic path we can sign off on quickly so Atlas can hit April onboarding: 

1) Provisional agreement (immediate):
   - Net45 payment terms for the first 60 days
   - Provisional credit limit: EUR 150,000
   - Condition: signed credit_application.pdf + PO for the first month (we’ll treat PO as commitment)

2) Full review (concurrent):
   - Once we receive the completed application and trade refs, Finance will complete a 7-business-day review.
   - If the review is positive, Redwood will move to Net60 and increase limit to EUR 200,000 (or higher subject to references).
   - If the review flags concerns we will provide a written explanation and alternative options (e.g., partial prepayment, milestone invoicing).

3) VAT handling:
   - With the supplied VAT ID and certificate we will apply reverse-charge on EU invoices. Please ensure the VAT ID appears on your PO.

4) Final documentation: attached provisional_terms.pdf outlines the above and contains the signature box for the credit app and PO acknowledgement.

We appreciate the urgency — if you return the completed credit_application.pdf and the PO by EOD Wed 11 Mar we’ll start the provisional Net45 and prioritize the review to meet your 15 Mar target.

Best,
Marissa
Attachment: provisional_terms.pdf (PDF, 45KB)


From: Sofia Alvarez <sofia@atlasfinance.eu>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 9 Mar 2026 15:40:00 +01:00

- Receive completed credit_application.pdf (attached template)
- Two trade references and last 3 months of topline/POs if available
- Typical decision window: 5–7 business days
- Possible credit limit: up to EUR 150k on initial approval

Option B — Provisional commit (temporary while review completes):
- We can approve a provisional credit line of EUR 100k for 30 days to allow onboarding (Net45), conditional on signed credit app and a PO for the first invoice
- If credit review completes positively before 30 days, we’ll increase to negotiated limit and move to Net60 if approved

Notes on VAT: as long as Atlas provides a valid VAT ID and evidence (VAT_certificate.pdf), we can apply reverse-charge treatment on EU invoices. Finance will require the VAT ID in the credit application and the PO to carry the VAT number.

title: Follow-up: token volumes, budget range & multi-year incentive options
preview:
From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
To: Sofia Morales <sofia@lumenhealth.ai>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-03-05T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Follow-up: token volumes, budget range & multi-year incentive options

Hi Sofia — thanks again for taking time yesterday. Quick recap and a couple of asks so we can get a disciplined quote back to you: 

Recap from call:
- Primary use: clinical triage assistant across chat and batch summarization for referral letters.
- Expected steady-state: 30M tokens / month in year 1, with peak bursts up to ~60M in month-end processing.
- Target unit economics: team is aiming for ~$800–$1,200 per 1M tokens (post-discount) as a decision threshold.

Questions & next steps we discussed:
1) Confirm target committed monthly tokens for pricing (you mentioned 30M steady, is that final?).
2) Budget cadence: is the $350k ARR budget range annual or a 12-month committed spend?
3) Interest in multi-year pricing (12 vs 24 month committed use) + startup program eligibility for an additional credit/discount?

If you can confirm the commit horizon and whether the CFO (I can see Evan copied) prefers monthly or annual billing, I’ll loop in finance and legal and send a short commitment tier with worked unit economics. I’ve attached a short commitment tier draft and an excel with example math for 1M/5M/30M volumes so you can eyeball the per-1M numbers.

Proposed timing: if we get numbers by EOD Monday, I’ll return a formal quote and a proposed 60%/40% draft for multi-year discount steps by Wednesday.

Thanks — Jonas
Jonas Weber
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
jonas.weber@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0192

Attachments: Redwood_Commitment_Tiers_Q1-2026.pdf (pdf, 2 pages); Estimated_Unit_Econ_1M_tokens.xlsx (xlsx, 1 sheet)


From: Sofia Morales <sofia@lumenhealth.ai>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Cc: Evan Cho <evan.cho@lumenhealth.ai>
Date: 2026-03-05T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up: token volumes, budget range & multi-year incentive options

Jonas — thanks for the clear recap and the attachments. A couple of clarifications from our side: 

- 30M/month steady-state is correct for year 1. We expect 2x grow in year 2 if the pilot goes well.
- The budget note ($350k) was annual; CFO wants committed-use pricing that maps to an annual forecast.
- We are open to committing to 24 months if the unit-costs get closer to ~$600–$700 / 1M tokens.

Can you also confirm: is the startup program an additive credit (applied once) or a recurring percent discount? Our procurement team will want that spelled out.

If you can get a proposal that shows 12m vs 24m committed tiers and the midpoint $/1M values, Evan and I will review and flag questions. Thanks — Sofia
Sofia Morales
Head of Product, Lumen Health
sofia@lumenhealth.ai


From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
To: Sofia Morales <sofia@lumenhealth.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Evan Cho <evan.cho@lumenhealth.ai>
Date: 2026-03-07T08:18:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up: token volumes, budget range & multi-year incentive options

Hi Sofia / Evan — looping in Monica (Finance) and Rafael (Contracts) to put formal numbers on paper. Below is a concise proposal (high level) and rationale; attached is the PDF + the spreadsheet with the line math so procurement can drop into their TCO review.

High-level proposal (assuming 30M/month steady, billed monthly, 12 vs 24 month committed use):
- No commit (on-demand): list price = $2,100 / 1M tokens.
- 12-month commit (360M tokens/year): committed unit price = $1,050 / 1M (50% off list)
- 24-month commit (720M tokens over 2 years): committed unit price = $780 / 1M (62.9% off list)
- Startup program: one-time onboarding credit = $25k applied in month 1 OR an alternative 5% recurring discount for eligible companies; we can pick depending on whether you prefer CAPEX-like credit vs. ongoing unit discount.

Example math (see attached spreadsheet):
- With 30M/month over 12 months (360M): at $1,050/1M = ~$378k annual spend (meets your ~$350k ARR tolerance after startup credit or minor negotiation).
- With 24-month commit at $780/1M: annual equivalent = ~$281k/yr (lower, but 2-year paid commitment).

Notes on flexibility and risks:
- We can structure the 24-month as a two-year commitment with quarterly usage true-up; shortfalls or overages are billed at the committed unit price for anything within a 10% band, with overage blocks at committed price + 15%.
- For the startup credit: if you prefer the 5% recurring instead of the $25k onboarding credit, Monica flagged that a recurring discount requires minor contractual language but is fine.

Next steps I propose: 
1) Sofia/Evan confirm preferred commit horizon (12 vs 24) and preference on startup credit form.
2) Monica to send a short commercial term sheet (we aim for 1 page) + Rafael to prepare an initial DPA/NDA + term sheet.
3) If confirm by Monday EOD we can provide a DocuSign-ready signature package and a price lock for 14 days.

Thanks — Jonas

---
Monica Patel (Finance) adds: We attached the commitment tiers PDF and the unit econ spreadsheet; happy to run a couple of sensitivity scenarios if you want to swap 30M for an alternate commit number.
Rafael Mendes (Legal) adds: We can draft a 2-page amendment with the 24m commit language and minimal termination/true-up clauses; will plan to keep it simple for procurement review.

Attachments: Redwood_Commitment_Tiers_Q1-2026.pdf; Estimated_Unit_Econ_1M_tokens.xlsx


From: Evan Cho <evan.cho@lumenhealth.ai>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-03-08T16:48:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up: token volumes, budget range & multi-year incentive options

Thanks Jonas / Monica / Rafael — this is exactly the level of detail we needed. Evan here (CFO) — we reviewed the numbers with procurement and prefer the 24-month commit at the $780 / 1M with the $25k onboarding credit instead of a recurring discount. That puts our forecast well under internal budget risk thresholds.

Please can you send the short commercial term sheet + DocuSign contract for signature? We aim to get this signed by the end of next week to kick off integration work.

Sofia/Evan (Lumen)

[Short acceptance — procurement will review the contract but is comfortable proceeding on the proposed commercial terms.]

title: Security questionnaire kickoff & clarifying items for FinBank POC
preview:
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Olivia Barnes <olivia.barnes@finbank.com>
Cc: Jake Cole <jake.cole@finbank.com>
Date: Mon, 02 Mar 2026 14:10:00 -0700
Subject: Security questionnaire kickoff & clarifying items for FinBank POC

Hi Olivia — thanks again for the call earlier today. Great to hear FinBank is moving quickly on the POC. Per our discussion, I’m attaching a blank SIG-format questionnaire (FinBank_SIG.xlsx) and a short SOC 2 summary PDF that outlines our scope and the most common evidence requests.

Quick recap and asks so we’re aligned:
- Goal: complete the vendor questionnaire and provide SOC 2 artifacts so your security team can greenlight the POC by the end of March.
- Attachments: FinBank_SIG.xlsx (SIG spreadsheet) and redwood-soc2-summary.pdf (one-page summary + evidence index).

Clarifying questions from our side so we can fill the SIG accurately:
1) Scope: Will the POC include production data or only synthetic/test data? (affects data residency/retention answers)
2) Compliance regimes: You mentioned GDPR in the call — can you confirm whether HIPAA controls are required for this engagement (is any PHI expected)?
3) Evidence preference: Does FinBank prefer a link to our audit portal (drive link) or discrete PDF artifacts attached to the SIG?
4) Subprocessors: Are there any redlines around cloud subprocessors (e.g., disallowing specific public cloud regions)?

If you can answer those four items, I’ll populate the SIG and then loop in our security & compliance leads (Marissa and Monica) for any follow-up. Proposed timeline: we aim to return a completed SIG + evidence bundle within 48 business hours after we receive your initial answers and any requested scope constraints.

If helpful, I’m available tomorrow 10–11am PT to walk through the worksheet live.

Thanks — looking forward to moving this forward.

Best,
Rafael Mendes
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0193
Redwood Inference

Attachments:
- FinBank_SIG.xlsx (SIG questionnaires tab)
- redwood-soc2-summary.pdf (SOC 2 summary, scope and evidence index)

From: Olivia Barnes <olivia.barnes@finbank.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jake Cole <jake.cole@finbank.com>
Date: Mon, 02 Mar 2026 17:02:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: Security questionnaire kickoff & clarifying items for FinBank POC

Hi Rafael — thanks for the materials and the clear recap. A few quick answers to your questions below, and I attached the first tab of our intake to this reply so you can see what our security team expects.

1) Scope: POC will use sanitized production-derived data (Pseudonymized dataset). No raw PHI expected for this initial engagement.
2) Compliance: GDPR applies (we process EU customer accounts), but HIPAA is not required for this POC.
3) Evidence preference: our InfoSec team prefers links to a secure evidence portal (SFTP or pre-authenticated drive link) for large evidence; smaller artifacts (PDFs) are fine directly in the SIG.
4) Subprocessors: no hard bans on mainstream cloud regions, but we’d like to avoid storing EU customer data in an APAC region for this POC.

Timeline: We need the completed SIG + evidence by EOD Friday (2026-03-05) to make the security review window.

I’m looping in Jake (security) who’ll be the reviewer if Rafael has follow-ups.

title: Encryption / KMS follow-up and next steps
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Anita Shah <anita.shah@brighthealthai.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 02 Mar 2026 09:04:00 -08:00
Subject: Encryption / KMS follow-up and next steps

Hi Anita / Tom —

Thanks again for the demo and the great discussion this morning. Quick recap from our side focused on the encryption, key management, and data residency questions that came up, plus proposed next steps so we can keep momentum: 

What we covered (short):
- In-transit: TLS 1.2+ enforced between client and Redwood edge, mutual TLS available for Dedicated/Private deployments.
- At-rest: Model payloads and persisted KV caches are AES-256 encrypted. Customer-managed keys (BYOK) via KMS are supported for Private and Dedicated; for Hosted we offer envelope encryption and can integrate with customer KMS via a dedicated HSM-backed key proxy for enterprise contracts.
- Key rotation & access: Rotation supported via KMS integrations; RBAC and audit logs are available in Redwood Console (token-level tracing + key access logs).
- Data residency & retention: You can pin workloads to region-specific clusters (us-west/us-east/eu) and set retention policies per project. We persist logs for the time window you specify; we do not retain application-level PII longer than the retention configured.

Questions you flagged for follow-up:
1) Can BrightHealth bring-your-own-key (BYOK) for the hosted offering, or do we need a Private deployment?
2) What does the audit trail look like for key usage (who requested unwrap/usage)?
3) Is HSM-backed signing/attestation available for key import and root-of-trust?
4) Any required contract language or SOC2/pen testing evidence we should provide for procurement?

Proposed next steps:
- Rafael to send a short tech note with KMS integration options and an architecture diagram (attachment).
- Camila to share a SOC 2 summary and our standard BYOK SOW template for review.
- Schedule a 30-min follow-up with BrightHealth infra team next week to align on KMS path (I propose Wed 2026-03-10 10:00 PT; please confirm).

Attachments in this message: redwood_kms_integration_guide.pdf (short doc) and kms-arch-diagram.png

Thanks — please let me know if I missed anything. Looking forward to aligning on the KMS approach.

Best,

--
Camila Reyes
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0119
redwoodinference.com


From: Anita Shah <anita.shah@brighthealthai.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@brighthealthai.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 02 Mar 2026 11:22:00 -06:00
Subject: Re: Encryption / KMS follow-up and next steps

Camila — thanks for the clear recap. A few clarifying items from our security and procurement teams: 

1) BYOK for hosted: our preference is to avoid a full Private deployment if possible. Is the HSM-backed key proxy you mentioned a managed integration where BrightHealth retains key material control? What are the limitations (latency, per-request cost, key rotation)?

2) Audit trail: we need to demonstrate to auditors that we can see a per-request unwrap/usage event including calling principal (service principal or user), timestamp, and operation type. Will Redwood supply logs suitable for ingestion into our SIEM (JSON lines preferred)?

3) HSM attestation: we need an attestation artifact proving hardware-backed key protection when keys are imported; if that requires Private deployment we need to know the delta vs Hosted.

4) Procurement: please attach the SOC2 report (redacted OK) and a short statement about physical access controls and key custodianship. Our legal will prepare standard DPIA questions as well.

Can Rafael weigh in on the BYOK proxy approach? Also — Wed 03/10 10:00 PT works for our infra lead.

Thanks,
Anita

--
Anita Shah
Head of IT Security | BrightHealth AI
anita.shah@brighthealthai.com | +1 (617) 555-2102


From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Anita Shah <anita.shah@brighthealthai.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 02 Mar 2026 12:43:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Encryption / KMS follow-up and next steps

Thanks Anita — quick technical clarifications below, happy to expand in the call next week.

BYOK for Hosted (HSM-backed proxy):
- Overview: For enterprise customers we offer a hosted BYOK flow where BrightHealth keeps root key material in your KMS/HSM. We exchange short-lived wrapping keys; an HSM-backed key proxy in our control plane performs envelope operations without ever storing plaintext root keys. This is similar to 'Bring Your Own Key' patterns used by major cloud vendors.
- Limitations: adds ~15-30ms median latency on token exchange paths for each new key unwrap operation (cached unwrap results in lower per-token latency). Per-request usage cost shows as a small operations surcharge in billing.
- Rotation: We support automated rotation via KMS APIs; rotations can be scheduled and rollback is supported for 7 days.

Audit trail & logs:
- We emit structured logs for key unwrap/usage events with fields: timestamp, operation, key_id (opaque), principal_id (internal service or token id), request_id. Logs are exportable as JSON-lines to customer S3/GCS or pushed to your SIEM via a secure forwarder.

HSM attestation:
- For Hosted + proxy: we can provide attestation proofs from our HSM vendor showing FIPS 140-2 Level 3 where applicable; importing customer keys into an HSM in our environment requires MOU and legal approvals.
- Private deployment delta: in Private, customer owns the HSM and key lifecycle fully — you get direct access to HSM attestation artifacts and physical custody controls.

Attachments: SOC2-Summary-2025.pdf (security summary), redwood_kms_integration_guide.pdf, kms-arch-diagram.png

Happy to walk through the SAML/mTLS auth flows during the call.

-Rafael

--
Rafael Mendes
Principal Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com | +1 (650) 555-0147

- Data residency & retention: You can pin workloads to region-specific clusters (us-west/us-east/eu) and set retention policies per project. We persist logs for the time window you specify; we do not retain application-level PII longer than the retention configured.

Questions you flagged for follow-up:
1) Can BrightHealth bring-your-own-key (BYOK) for the hosted offering, or do we need a Private deployment?
2) What does the audit trail look like for key usage (who requested unwrap/usage)?
3) Is HSM-backed signing/attestation available for key import and root-of-trust?
4) Any required contract language or SOC2/pen testing evidence we should provide for procurement?

Proposed next steps:
- Rafael to send a short tech note with KMS integration options and an architecture diagram (attachment).
- Camila to share a SOC 2 summary and our standard BYOK SOW template for review.
- Schedule a 30-min follow-up with BrightHealth infra team next week to align on KMS path (I propose Wed 2026-03-10 10:00 PT; please confirm).

Camila — thanks for the clear recap. A few clarifying items from our security and procurement teams: 

1) BYOK for hosted: our preference is to avoid a full Private deployment if possible. Is the HSM-backed key proxy you mentioned a managed integration where BrightHealth retains key material control? What are the limitations (latency, per-request cost, key rotation)?

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Anita Shah <anita.shah@brighthealthai.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 02 Mar 2026 12:43:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Encryption / KMS follow-up and next steps

Thanks Anita — quick technical clarifications below, happy to expand in the call next week.

BYOK for Hosted (HSM-backed proxy):
- Overview: For enterprise customers we offer a hosted BYOK flow where BrightHealth keeps root key material in your KMS/HSM. We exchange short-lived wrapping keys; an HSM-backed key proxy in our control plane performs envelope operations without ever storing plaintext root keys. This is similar to 'Bring Your Own Key' patterns used by major cloud vendors.
- Limitations: adds ~15-30ms median latency on token exchange paths for each new key unwrap operation (cached unwrap results in lower per-token latency). Per-request usage cost shows as a small operations surcharge in billing.
- Rotation: We support automated rotation via KMS APIs; rotations can be scheduled and rollback is supported for 7 days.

Audit trail & logs:
- We emit structured logs for key unwrap/usage events with fields: timestamp, operation, key_id (opaque), principal_id (internal service or token id), request_id. Logs are exportable as JSON-lines to customer S3/GCS or pushed to your SIEM via a secure forwarder.

HSM attestation:
- For Hosted + proxy: we can provide attestation proofs from our HSM vendor showing FIPS 140-2 Level 3 where applicable; importing customer keys into an HSM in our environment requires MOU and legal approvals.
- Private deployment delta: in Private, customer owns the HSM and key lifecycle fully — you get direct access to HSM attestation artifacts and physical custody controls.

Attachments: SOC2-Summary-2025.pdf (security summary), redwood_kms_integration_guide.pdf, kms-arch-diagram.png

title: Clarifying data roles and execution path for privacy addendum
preview:
From: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@zenithhealth.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
Cc: Carlos Ortega <c.ortega@zenithhealth.com>, Zenith Health Legal <legal@zenithhealth.com>
Date: Thu, 12 Feb 2026 14:23:00 -0500
Subject: Questions on the DPA draft: roles, logs, and signing flow

Hi Naomi,

Thanks for sending the DPA draft earlier today. Our counsel reviewed and had three quick clarifying asks before we route signatures:

1) Role determination: the draft sometimes references \"service provider\" and sometimes \"processor/controller\" in the same paragraph. We need an explicit clause that states when Zenith is a controller vs. Redwood is a processor (and vice versa) for specific feature flows. Can you suggest a short paragraph we can add?

2) Operational telemetry & logs: we want to understand what logs Redwood retains that could contain user-identifying metadata (request headers, client IPs, request body previews). We also need proposed retention periods for those logs and a way to request deletion for a particular account subject.

3) Signing: will you be sending a DocuSign invite for the final redline or do you prefer AdobeSign? Our procurement prefers DocuSign but legal is flexible.

I attached our annotated DPA. Happy to hop on a 30m call if that helps.

Best,
Elena Ruiz
Head of Privacy, Zenith Health

[attachment: zenith-proposed-dpa-redline.docx]

From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@zenithhealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 12 Feb 2026 16:05:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: Questions on the DPA draft: roles, logs, and signing flow

Elena — thanks for the clear asks. Looping in Laura from Legal and Diego from Product so we can provide concrete language.

Short answers up front:
- We can add a concise \"Roles and Scope\" clause that maps feature sets to controller/processor roles. Proposed text below in a separate email.
- We retain request metadata (timestamp, route, model_id, truncated request preview) and operational logs (worker traces, kernel error traces). We do not store raw request bodies for longer than 30 days by default in hosted plans — product can propose longer retention for Dedicated/Private.
- DocuSign preferred by us as well; we will send an invitation once you confirm redline agreement.

I'll ask Legal for suggested language and Product for a brief retention table that we can include in an exhibit.

—Naomi
Director, Customer Privacy & Trust
Redwood Inference

\"\"

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 13 Feb 2026 09:18:00 -08:00
Subject: Proposed \"Roles and Scope\" clause + telemetry exhibit

Naomi/Diego — draft language below that I think addresses Zenith's ask while keeping our risk profile tight. Please review and flag edits.

Proposed clause (to insert under Definitions/Controller & Processor):
\"\"\"
1. Controller vs Processor. For the purposes of this Agreement: (a) Zenith Health will act as Controller with respect to Personal Data that Zenith submits to the Service for the purposes of receiving inference, analytics, or other features; (b) Redwood Inference will act as Processor with respect to such Personal Data and will process it only on documented instructions from Zenith. Notwithstanding the foregoing, where Redwood introduces platform-generated telemetry or aggregated usage metrics that do not contain Personal Data (as defined herein), Redwood will be Controller of such telemetry.
\"\"\"

Telemetry/Operational Logs Exhibit (summary):
- Request metadata: timestamp, model identifier, route — retained 90 days (hosted); customizable for Dedicated/Private
- Request previews: truncated to 512 bytes for debugging, retained 30 days
- Access logs (authentication events): retained 1 year for security auditing
- Kernel/worker traces: retained 30 days; error dumps retained 180 days for debugging when explicitly requested via support ticket

Deletion and access: Redwood will provide an account-level portal to request deletion of retained request previews and will honor verified deletion requests within 30 days for hosted accounts. For Dedicated/Private customers with on-prem settings, deletion timelines are as per the deployment configuration.

Notes: this keeps the default hosted retention conservative while allowing Dedicated customers to set custom policies. Rafael — please confirm whether we should shorten any retention windows to reduce risk.

—Laura
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference

[attachment: redwood-proposed-privacy-exhibit.pdf]

From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@zenithhealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Feb 2026 11:02:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Questions on the DPA draft: roles, logs, and signing flow

Elena — attaching the language Laura proposed and a short exhibit with retention windows for Hosted vs Dedicated. Diego added a note on \"derived vectors\" (our internal term) — those are treated as non-identifying telemetry unless you submit raw PII fields within prompt text, in which case they are handled as Personal Data.

We can proceed in one of two ways:
1) You accept the attached redline and we send a DocuSign envelope for execution.
2) We schedule a 30m call to walk through the exhibit and retention values.

I've included a PDF preview of the DocuSign envelope we'll send once you confirm; docket reference: ZEN-DPA-002.

Thanks,
Naomi
Director, Customer Privacy & Trust
Redwood Inference

[attachment: docusign-invite.pdf]

From: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@zenithhealth.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
Cc: Zenith Health Legal <legal@zenithhealth.com>, Carlos Ortega <c.ortega@zenithhealth.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Feb 2026 10:05:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Questions on the DPA draft: roles, logs, and signing flow

Naomi/Laura — thanks for the quick turnaround. Zenith is comfortable with the Roles and Scope language as drafted and the retention table generally looks reasonable. Two small follow-ups before signature:

• Can you add a sentence confirming that request previews are excluded from exports in the admin portal unless explicitly requested (we want to ensure we don't inadvertently receive truncated previews as part of audit exports).
• We prefer DocuSign. Please send the envelope and copy legal@zenithhealth.com on the signing notification.

Once those two items are addressed we'll sign. Appreciate the clarity here — happy to close this week.

Best,
Elena
Head of Privacy, Zenith Health

From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@zenithhealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 12 Feb 2026 16:05:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: Questions on the DPA draft: roles, logs, and signing flow

Elena — thanks for the clear asks. Looping in Laura from Legal and Diego from Product so we can provide concrete language.

Short answers up front:
- We can add a concise \"Roles and Scope\" clause that maps feature sets to controller/processor roles. Proposed text below in a separate email.
- We retain request metadata (timestamp, route, model_id, truncated request preview) and operational logs (worker traces, kernel error traces). We do not store raw request bodies for longer than 30 days by default in hosted plans — product can propose longer retention for Dedicated/Private.
- DocuSign preferred by us as well; we will send an invitation once you confirm redline agreement.

I'll ask Legal for suggested language and Product for a brief retention table that we can include in an exhibit.

—Naomi
Director, Customer Privacy & Trust
Redwood Inference

\"\"

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 13 Feb 2026 09:18:00 -08:00
Subject: Proposed \"Roles and Scope\" clause + telemetry exhibit

Naomi/Diego — draft language below that I think addresses Zenith's ask while keeping our risk profile tight. Please review and flag edits.

Proposed clause (to insert under Definitions/Controller & Processor):
\"\"\"
1. Controller vs Processor. For the purposes of this Agreement: (a) Zenith Health will act as Controller with respect to Personal Data that Zenith submits to the Service for the purposes of receiving inference, analytics, or other features; (b) Redwood Inference will act as Processor with respect to such Personal Data and will process it only on documented instructions from Zenith. Notwithstanding the foregoing, where Redwood introduces platform-generated telemetry or aggregated usage metrics that do not contain Personal Data (as defined herein), Redwood will be Controller of such telemetry.
\"\"\"

Telemetry/Operational Logs Exhibit (summary):
- Request metadata: timestamp, model identifier, route — retained 90 days (hosted); customizable for Dedicated/Private
- Request previews: truncated to 512 bytes for debugging, retained 30 days
- Access logs (authentication events): retained 1 year for security auditing
- Kernel/worker traces: retained 30 days; error dumps retained 180 days for debugging when explicitly requested via support ticket

Deletion and access: Redwood will provide an account-level portal to request deletion of retained request previews and will honor verified deletion requests within 30 days for hosted accounts. For Dedicated/Private customers with on-prem settings, deletion timelines are as per the deployment configuration.

Notes: this keeps the default hosted retention conservative while allowing Dedicated customers to set custom policies. Rafael — please confirm whether we should shorten any retention windows to reduce risk.

—Laura
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference

title: RAG evaluation follow-up & proposed action plan
preview:
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
To: Amelia Chen <amelia@zenon.ai>, Samir Gupta <samir.gupta@zenon.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Feb 2026 23:10:00 +0000
Subject: RAG evaluation follow-up & proposed action plan

Hi Amelia / Samir —

Thanks again for the productive discovery call earlier today. Quick recap of the items we agreed and a proposed next-step plan focused on validating model fit + RAG settings before a pilot: 

Summary of needs (from the call)
- Use case: internal knowledge base Q&A + contract summarization for legal ops (mix of long documents and short policy pages).
- Primary success metrics: grounding precision (we heard ~95% target), response latency per query (<400ms median), and low hallucination on contract-specific prompts.
- Data: ~120k documents, mix of PDFs and HTML; currently indexed in Elasticsearch.

Our recommended evaluation plan (MVP)
1) Small benchmark run (2k queries): we will run RAG permutations to measure grounding precision and model-level quality.
   - Candidate generator models: Redwood-1.5 (recommended default), Llama-3-13B-q4 (cost-optimized baseline).
   - Embedding model: text-embedding-3-large for dense retrieval.
2) Retriever settings to test:
   - Dense top_k: 50 (baseline), 100 (aggressive).
   - Hybrid scoring: dense_score * 0.8 + sparse_score * 0.2 (we’ll convert your ES BM25 output to a sparse score).
3) Reranker options:
   - Cross-encoder reranker v2 (ours) — higher precision, higher latency (use for canary).
   - Lightweight candidate filter (bi-encoder) — much cheaper, slightly lower precision (for production scaling).
4) Prompt sets & evals:
   - We’ll run three prompt variants: conservative grounding-first, concise-answer, and extractive-summaries.
   - Please find attached our starter prompt set (RAG_eval_prompt_set_v1.csv).

Deliverables and timing
- Pre-run: mapping and ingest check (you provide sample exported docs + ES query examples) — 2 business days.
- Benchmark run (2k queries, 3 model+retriever permutations) — 3 business days.
- Analysis report with grounding metrics, token economics, and recommended production config — 2 business days.

Next steps we need from you
1) Share 500 representative documents (PDFs or exported text) and 200 example user queries (we can use a HubSpot file or Drive link).
2) Confirm whether we can temporarily snapshot a small ES index (or provide nightly export).
3) Share any security/compliance requirements for the eval (e.g., IP allowlist, data residency).

Attachments: RAG_eval_prompt_set_v1.csv (prompt set), benchmark_report_prerun.pdf (proposal), retriever_config_v2.json (example mapping)

I’ve CC’d Monica and Logan — Monica will own the reranker/retriever config and Logan will help with the cost/throughput estimates.

If this looks good I’ll schedule the pre-run for Monday and we’ll kick off immediately after we get the samples.

Best,
Rafael Mendes
Enterprise Solutions Lead
Redwood Inference
rafael.mendes@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0189



From: Amelia Chen <amelia@zenon.ai>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Cc: Samir Gupta <samir.gupta@zenon.ai>
Date: Fri, 20 Feb 2026 00:45:00 +0000
Subject: Re: RAG evaluation follow-up & proposed action plan

Hi Rafael —

Thanks for the thorough recap. A few clarifying questions before we hand over the samples: 

1) For the 2k query benchmark, do you recommend we hand over labeled ground-truth for all q/a pairs or will you run automated checks plus a human spot-check?
2) On the reranker — curious how latency will be measured if we use cross-encoder only for final scoring. Will that still meet the <400ms median target?
3) About embeddings: we currently use an older in-house embedding. If we switch to text-embedding-3-large for the test, what is the estimated embedding cost for 120k docs (ballpark)?

We can share the 500 docs by Monday. For ES snapshoting: we prefer a nightly export — would that be sufficient for your team? Also, we need to check with our security team about a temporary inbound allowlist.

Amelia
Head of Product
Zenon AI
amelia@zenon.ai



From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Feb 2026 01:20:00 +0000
Subject: Re: RAG evaluation follow-up & proposed action plan

Rafael / Logan — quick technical notes for the benchmark and what I’ll run once we have the samples. Keeping it concise for the external thread.

Bench config (will version in retriever_config_v2.json)
- Dense retriever: embedding model = text-embedding-3-large; index with faiss/HNSW. Top_k candidates = [50, 100].
- Sparse signals: BM25 from ES; normalize BM25 to 0-1 and combine as hybrid_score = 0.8 * dense_score + 0.2 * sparse_score. We’ll also try 0.9/0.1 as a sensitivity check.
- Reranker: two flows
   a) Production-light: bi-encoder candidate filter (use sentence-transformers) + generator with Redwood-1.5. Expected grounding precision ~88-92%.
   b) High-precision canary: cross-encoder reranker v2 applied to the top 50 candidates — expect precision uplift to ~93-97% depending on prompt set and context window. Latency tradeoff: single-query median expected to increase ~80-140ms for the reranker pass (depends on batch/concurrency).

Prompt engineering notes
- Use explicit grounding instruction: “Cite the source paragraph and confidence estimate. If not found, respond: ‘No grounded answer found.’”
- For extractive summarization prompts, limit generation to 250 tokens to reduce hallucination risk.

Grounding metrics to report (what we’ll calculate)
- Grounding precision: fraction of model answers that include a correct cited source.
- Hallucination rate: fraction of answers with unsupported factual assertions (human-audited sample).
- Retrieval recall@k: fraction of ground-truth passages present in top_k.
- Token economics: cost per query at 95th, median, and p99.

Data & infra ask
- We need the 500 docs as plaintext or PDF and 200 queries with expected answers if available.
- If ES export is possible, include 1:1 mapping of doc IDs to source (helps validate citations).

I’ll prepare two config permutations and the scripts to run the reranker/UDFs. Rafael — once you get the samples I’ll start the pre-run.

— Monica
Senior ML Engineer, Retrieval & Rerank
monica.patel@redwood.com



From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
To: Amelia Chen <amelia@zenon.ai>
Cc: Samir Gupta <samir.gupta@zenon.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Feb 2026 02:37:00 +0000
Subject: Re: RAG evaluation follow-up & proposed action plan

Amelia —

Perfect — thanks for the quick response. To answer your questions quickly: 
1) Labeled ground truth: ideal but not strictly necessary. We can run automated checks + a 200-sample human audit you provide. If you can supply labels for ~500 queries that will speed threshold decisions.
2) Cross-encoder latency: Monica’s note above is accurate — adding the reranker will add ~80-140ms to the median depending on batching. We’ll measure p50/p95 and if the reranker costs push beyond your SLA, we can move to a hybrid: run cross-encoder on a sample of high-uncertainty queries only.
3) Embedding cost: rough estimate for 120k docs on text-embedding-3-large: ~ $850–$1,200 one-time for the initial indexing (ballpark; we’ll provide a precise quote). Logan will send a detailed cost breakdown after the pre-run.

Action items (owners & timing)
- Zenon: share 500 docs + 200 queries by Monday (Amelia) — owner: Zenon product.
- Redwood: run pre-run mapping and a 2k query benchmark (Monica) — start within 24 hours of receiving samples.
- Redwood: deliver analysis report with recommended production config and cost estimate (Logan) — 5 business days after benchmark completes.

If that aligns I’ll schedule the pre-run slot for Monday 02/23 and create the HubSpot deal activity (link above).

Thanks — looking forward to moving to the pilot phase.

Best,
Rafael
Enterprise Solutions Lead
Redwood Inference
rafael.mendes@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0189

title: Cross-border invoicing / request for self-billing + VAT/GST treatment
preview:
From: Sofia Kaur <sofia.kaur@greenlinelogistics.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Cc: APAC Tax Team <tax@greenlinelogistics.com>, Accounts Payable <ap@greenlinelogistics.com>
Date: 2026-02-11T09:12:00-05:00
Subject: Request: self-billing & VAT/GST routing for Redwood invoices

Hi Kimberly,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday — Greenline is ready to move forward but we have a few billing constraints we need to confirm before the PO is issued. Our tax and AP teams asked whether Redwood can support a self-billing arrangement so our AP team issues payment and we receive Redwood invoices that are net of VAT under EU reverse-charge where applicable. High-level requirements from our side: 

- EU customers (Ireland HQ) will provide validated VAT IDs; they expect Redwood to issue invoices net of VAT under reverse-charge (customer liability).
- Singapore subsidiary: services are exported and should be zero-rated GST; we can provide Singapore GST registration if needed.
- U.S. parent: no sales tax for Redwood services in states where Greenline has no nexus; if any state has nexus, Greenline will remit sales tax and provide certificates.

Can Redwood: 
1) Accept self-billing (we can sign an agreement)
2) Route the invoices per legal entity and apply reverse-charge / zero-rate where appropriate
3) Generate invoices to our AP mailbox with a PDF copy to Sofia for PO reconciliation

We can attach a sample W-8 and VAT proof if that helps.

Thanks,
Sofia Kaur
Head of Procurement
Greenline Logistics
[Attachment: sample_company_vat_cert.pdf]


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Sofia Kaur <sofia.kaur@greenlinelogistics.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-02-11T10:03:00-05:00
Subject: Re: Request: self-billing & VAT/GST routing for Redwood invoices

Hi Sofia — thanks for the clear summary and for being proactive. Short answer: we can support a self-billing flow and multi-entity invoice routing, but I want to confirm a couple of operational items with Finance and Legal (cc'd).

Marcus / Laura / Rafael — can you confirm the following: 

- Redwood's self-billing policy: we require a signed self-billing agreement, VAT ID validation for EU reverse-charge, and entity-level mapping in our billing system.
- For Singapore GST zero-rating we need the GST registration number and a statement that services are exported.
- For U.S. sales tax we will not charge tax unless there is a documented nexus / resale certificate; Greenline will need to supply any relevant certificates.

Sofia — could you please send: (1) the VAT ID for the EU entity; (2) Singapore GST number if available; (3) remit instructions for AP (ACH/SEPA/SWIFT) and contact for DocuSign? We’ll draft the self-billing agreement and push it through DocuSign once Legal signs off.

I’ll follow up with a proposed timeline.

Best,
Kimberly Park
Account Executive, Redwood Inference


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Sofia Kaur <sofia.kaur@greenlinelogistics.com>
Date: 2026-02-12T14:21:00-05:00
Subject: RE: Request: self-billing & VAT/GST routing for Redwood invoices

Kimberly / Sofia — quick notes from Finance: 

1) Self-billing: we can do this but need a standard self-billing agreement signed and a matching mapping table (Greenline legal entity -> Redwood billing entity -> invoice language/PO reference). Please use our template (I'll attach).

2) EU reverse-charge: we will accept customer's validated VAT ID and issue invoices net of VAT, provided the VAT ID verifies via VIES and we retain the verification screenshot in the file. If VAT ID fails, we must charge VAT by default.

3) Singapore (GST): zero-rating will be applied only if we have the GST registration and a written export of services statement from Greenline. Singapore requires evidence.

4) U.S. sales tax: Redwood does not automatically apply state sales tax; we rely on customer-supplied resale/exemption certificates. If a state requires us to collect, Finance will coordinate with Legal and notify the customer before the first taxed invoice.

5) Payment rails: prefer SEPA for EUR, ACH for USD, and SWIFT for cross-border where necessary. Please include beneficiary details.

Attachments I will send: redwood_selfbilling_template.docx (for legal), instructions_for_vat_screenshots.pdf, and our W-9/W-8 acceptance checklist.

Rafael — can Legal confirm whether the self-billing template needs modification for EU customers?

Thanks,
Marcus
Director, Finance Operations
[Attachment: redwood_selfbilling_template.docx]

From: Sofia Kaur <sofia.kaur@greenlinelogistics.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Cc: APAC Tax Team <tax@greenlinelogistics.com>, Accounts Payable <ap@greenlinelogistics.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-02-13T08:07:00+00:00
Subject: Re: Request: self-billing & VAT/GST routing for Redwood invoices

Hi all,

Thanks Marcus — attaching the requested items. Quick answers inline: 

- EU VAT ID (Ireland HQ): IE3416789A (attached screenshot from VIES)
- Singapore GST#: 2019-102345 (attached GST cert) — services are exported, will sign export statement as part of the self-billing agreement
- U.S.: Greenline confirms no nexus in Redwood billing states; will provide any resale certificates if that changes.
- Payment rails / AP contact: AP will pay via SEPA for EUR invoices and ACH for USD. Beneficiary details attached. DocuSign contact: ap@greenlinelogistics.com

Attachments: W-8BEN-E.pdf, vat_validation_screenshot.png, self_billing_agreement.docx (marked up by our legal team).

Please confirm receipt and next steps.

Best,
Sofia
[Attachments: W-8BEN-E.pdf, vat_validation_screenshot.png, self_billing_agreement.docx]


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
