- They explicitly asked: “Are we going to get credits? We had a customer-visible incident.”
- They also asked whether they should pin traffic away from us-east for now.

My suggestions for the email:
- Include one sentence recommending temporary routing away from us-east *only if* they can do so without violating their internal requirements. Otherwise, we keep the guidance focused on retries/timeouts.
- Re: credits: agree with Sean — don’t promise. But we should reassure we’ll respond quickly if they submit a request.
- Update cadence: we told them we’d send a written update this morning and another check-in by EOD if anything changes. That’s aligned with what they expect.

Also: please keep wording very explicit that the workaround guidance is temporary and we’ll follow up with long-term prevention.

Rachel



From: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Feb 7, 2026 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Re: Exec escalation: Acme AI — streaming timeouts/disconnects (us-east) — approval needed

Adding Sales POV.

Acme is in the middle of expanding usage this quarter. If we go too “legalistic” on credits, we may take a hit in the expansion motion.

Question: are we comfortable with a *goodwill* credit (separate from contractual service credits) once stabilized, e.g., a one-time usage credit applied to next invoice, pending Jasmine approval?

I’m aligned with not committing in writing today. But if Morgan pushes on the call, I’d like a clear internal decision tree:
- If they ask verbally: do we say “we’ll evaluate and get back by X date” (what date)?
- Who signs off (Jasmine vs. Finance)?

Avery



From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Feb 7, 2026 at 10:19 AM PST
Subject: Re: Exec escalation: Acme AI — streaming timeouts/disconnects (us-east) — approval needed

Thanks all — incorporating edits now.

Decision / approvals:
1) Comms language: approved with Sean + Rafael’s edits.
- We will NOT state “root cause confirmed” or “permanently fixed.”
- We WILL state: mitigation applied, streaming stability metrics back to baseline, continued monitoring, RCA to follow.
- We WILL include temporary mitigation guidance (timeouts/retry; optional HTTP/1.1 note) and keep it explicitly “temporary.”

2) Credits:
- We will not proactively offer credits in today’s email.
- Verbal guidance if pressed: “We take this seriously and will evaluate service credits per your agreement once we finalize the incident timeline and impact assessment. We’ll get back to you by Wednesday, Feb 11.”
- Any goodwill credit beyond contractual service credits requires my approval; loop me + Avery.

Rachel: please send the finalized email to Morgan once I drop it into this thread (I’ll forward the final text in ~15 min). Rafael/Sean: thank you — please keep an eye on us-east streaming metrics through the afternoon and post in SUP-1842 if anything moves.

Jasmine

—
Jasmine Liu
VP, Customer Success & Support
Redwood Inference

title: Re: SRE sign-off: Support SLA thresholds + escalation triggers (Playbook refresh)
preview:
From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 6, 2026 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: SRE sign-off needed: Support SLA thresholds + escalation triggers (Playbook refresh)

Hey Sean,

We’re locking the SLA policy + escalation triggers today so we can publish Confluence and wire up Jira automations (INT-333) without churn.

Can you take a pass on:
1) proposed Support SLAs by tier (first response + update cadence + escalation thresholds)
2) how the thresholds map to SRE paging + existing SLO alerting

Goal is to avoid any language that implies we page SRE outside of what on-call is actually set up to handle.

Attachments:
- Redwood Support SLA Policy - Draft v3.pdf
- Escalation Playbook - Severity Matrix v2.pdf

If you can reply with “approved” or edits by EOD, that’d unblock publishing.

Thanks,
Dev

—
Dev Patel
Support Ops Lead
Redwood Inference



From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 6, 2026 at 10:03 AM PST
Subject: Re: SRE sign-off needed: Support SLA thresholds + escalation triggers (Playbook refresh)

Thanks Dev — read through. We’re close, but I can’t sign off as-is because some of the phrasing will cause “page SRE for every P1” behavior that doesn’t match our paging policy or our current alerting.

Requested edits (blocking):

A) Separate “Support update cadence” from “SRE paging threshold” explicitly.
- Draft currently implies: enterprise updates every 30 min == page on-call immediately.
- That’s not how we operate. We page based on customer impact + service health signals, not the comms cadence.

Concrete text suggestion:
- Add a sentence under the SLA table: “Update cadence is owned by Support/CS and may occur while Engineering investigation is pending; paging is triggered by service-impact criteria below.”

B) P0/P1 definitions need to reference measurable impact signals.
Right now the examples are good, but the triggers are too subjective (“high revenue risk”, “multiple customers complaining”).

Please add 2-3 objective triggers that are easy for Support to cite in a page:
- Error-rate threshold sustained for 10+ minutes (e.g., 5xx or streaming disconnect classification above baseline)
- Region-level latency degradation relative to SLO (p95) sustained for 10+ minutes
- Single enterprise/dedicated/private customer completely blocked (auth outage, total unavailability)

C) After-hours policy must match coverage reality.
The draft says “Dedicated/Private: page SRE after-hours for P1 within 60 minutes.”
- That will create paging noise and “please investigate my integration issue at 2am” pages.

Suggested language:
- After-hours: only page SRE for P0 or for P1 that meets the objective service-impact triggers.
- Otherwise Support acknowledges + schedules next-business-day Eng handoff.

D) Don’t put a 30-minute update cadence as a blanket promise.
I know the intent is to be responsive, but we’ll fail it in any complex incident. If we want 30 min updates for a subset (e.g., exec escalations), it needs an “if materially new info OR at least hourly” carve-out.

Non-blocking but recommended:
- Add a “Waiting on Customer” note: SLA timers pause only for first response/update cadence if we’ve asked for specific artifacts and the customer hasn’t responded; we should be careful with the word “pause” in customer-facing language. Keep it internal.

If you want, I can do a redline in the doc, but the above is enough to update the Confluence version.

Sean

—
Sean Gallagher
Site Reliability Engineering
Redwood Inference



From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 6, 2026 at 10:27 AM PST
Subject: Re: SRE sign-off needed: Support SLA thresholds + escalation triggers (Playbook refresh)

+1 to Sean’s points.

Two extra details to keep us aligned with on-call:

1) Paging criteria should include “customer is blocked” explicitly for Private upgrades and auth.
- Example: Private installer stuck at migrations in production upgrade window = page the Private on-call / SRE if rollback guidance doesn’t resolve within 30–60 min.

2) We should avoid “page SRE to confirm dashboards” language.
Support can use the Support-safe dashboards (once ENG-4823 lands), but pages should include:
- region
- model or endpoint
- time window
- 2–3 request IDs
- what changed (SDK upgrade, config change, deploy)

If we capture that in the template, pages get answered faster.

Rafael

—
Rafael Mendes
SRE On-Call / Incident Response
Redwood Inference



From: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 6, 2026 at 11:05 AM PST
Subject: Re: SRE sign-off needed: Support SLA thresholds + escalation triggers (Playbook refresh)

This is helpful. Two questions so I can align the enterprise language without overpromising:

1) For enterprise customers, can we keep “every 30 minutes” as an internal target for P0 only, and “hourly or when materially new info is available” for P1/P2?

2) For “Waiting on Customer,” we’ve had issues where Support keeps updating to avoid an SLA miss even when we’re blocked on logs. Are you OK with:
- internal SLA timer reminder continues, but update cadence becomes “next update after customer provides requested artifacts” (with a minimum check-in every X hours during business time)?

If that framing works, I’ll push edits into the policy language.

Rachel

—
Rachel Kim
Enterprise Customer Success Lead
Redwood Inference



From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 6, 2026 at 11:34 AM PST
Subject: Re: SRE sign-off needed: Support SLA thresholds + escalation triggers (Playbook refresh)

1) Yes — that’s aligned.
- P0: 30 min updates is fine as an internal target, but I’d still phrase it as “at least every 30 minutes while actively investigating/mitigating” (so if we’re in a stable monitoring state, hourly is acceptable).
- P1/P2: “hourly or when materially new info is available” is the right default.

2) Yes, but be careful with “next update after customer provides artifacts” as a blanket statement.
I’d suggest:
- Support continues to acknowledge at a steady cadence during business hours (e.g., every 4 hours) confirming we’re blocked and restating what’s needed.
- For enterprise P0/P1, do not go fully silent. Even if we’re blocked, we should provide “no new update; still awaiting X; here’s what we’ll do once received.”

And +1: keep the “pause SLA timers” concept internal only.

Sean



From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 6, 2026 at 4:38 PM PST
Subject: Re: SRE sign-off needed: Support SLA thresholds + escalation triggers (Playbook refresh)

Dev — assuming you incorporate the edits below, SRE signs off.

Sign-off conditions (please confirm in the published version):

1) Add explicit statement: Support SLA update cadence != paging threshold.
2) Add objective escalation triggers (error-rate/latency sustained; customer blocked) and put them above subjective triggers.
3) After-hours language: page SRE only for P0 or P1 that meets objective service-impact criteria.
4) Update cadence wording:
- P0: at least every 30 min while actively investigating/mitigating (hourly acceptable in stable monitoring)
- P1+: hourly or when materially new info is available
5) Template for pages includes required context (region, endpoint/model, time window, request IDs, what changed).

If you want a quick sanity check after you edit, paste the updated table and the after-hours paragraph in reply and I’ll do a final read.

Sean

—
Sean Gallagher
Site Reliability Engineering
Redwood Inference
